Q:

What is the best way to add a click handler to an element?

Just a quick question, what is the best practice for adding a handler to a DOM element?
A little background on the question:
In one of the projects I work on people have asked to create a "favorites" type of functionality. The idea being you could drop a bunch of items in your favorites list and then from within the app they could click on that favorite and it would take them to a list view. Since I've never built this type of application I'm having to figure out how to add the ability for them to "favor" an item.
I have created a div that will contain the favorite icon, and an li to represent the items being favorited. 
I have also created a class to handle the click event for the li element and the div where they can favorite it. I'm just wondering about best practice for handling a class of an element that was created dynamically when the user is clicking another element.

Should I create a variable somewhere and then add a selector to it, something like
var faveDiv = $("#faveDiv");
faveDiv.click(function(){ /*... */ }

With the continued growth in the complexity of modern computer systems, there is a growing need in the computer industry for more effective methods of interconnection between such systems. Specifically, many of today""s computer networks, such as local area networks (LANs), are widely used, both to transfer data between networked computer systems, as well as to pass signals between such systems. In particular, it is known to use network address translation (xe2x80x9cNATxe2x80x9d) address translation systems to regulate such accesses. Spatial analysis of community structure and diversity of soil bacterial communities across land-use gradients.
Environmental conditions can have profound effects on soil bacterial communities. Here, we have analyzed relationships among land-use, environmental, geographical, and biological aspects in predicting soil bacterial diversity and community structure. The objective of our research was to test the hypothesis that the composition and structure of the soil bacterial microbiome is not only strongly influenced by environmental factors, but that land-use is also a determinant of the structure and composition of soil bacterial communities. The study was conducted in the northern and southern hemispheres based on analysis of 16S rRNA gene bacterial communities using high-throughput sequence analysis. Our results show that land use and soil properties significantly affected bacterial community composition and diversity, but that geographic effects played a substantial, though not significant, role in bacterial community structure and diversity. Principal component analysis, permutational multivariate analysis of variance, correlation analysis, and Mantel tests showed that soil parameters significantly impacted both bacterial community composition and diversity. These results emphasize the importance of accounting for a combination of both geographical and ecological factors in ecological studies.

Qantas and Emirates are the latest airlines that have opted for a high-tech approach to their inflight entertainment systems.
According to a report published by Bloomberg, the carriers have begun testing new AirTV units that will be offered to passengers from the middle of 2014.
As Bloomberg notes, the units are being tested on Qantas’ Boeing 777-300ER aircraft and the Airbus A380-800, and are intended to provide access to live TV programs via onboard Wi-Fi.

The reports indicate that the units will be able to stream at least two video streams, including one for Sky News and a second that will give customers access to selected content on other sources such as Sony Crackle or Yahoo Sports.
Other content in the Sky program will reportedly come from channels Sky News and Fetch. The new Sky service will apparently be bundled with the Sky News Radio service.

The Air Emirates inflight entertainment system will reportedly give all passengers access to at least two programs, and possibly up to five, through a touchscreen or wireless remote control.
The system will also reportedly allow passengers to buy movies and videos as downloads from the airline’s partner studio, Filmcow. Emirates, which has its corporate headquarters in Dubai,

Q:

Pandoc: How to preserve newlines in Markdown

I have two strings in Markdown I am trying to convert into a pdf:

This is a test
The result is this

When my pandoc function gets them:
\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
$x = y$\n
\\
$ x = y$\n
\end{document}

I get the pdf like so:
This is a test
\begin{center}
$x = y$\n
\\

$ x = y$\n
\end{center}

The result is this

How can I preserve the newlines in Markdown when rendering the PDF?

A:

Pandoc's default behavior is to process each Markdown line as if the line was passed through Pandoc's own input filter, which filters out newlines but doesn't preserve them. This is what you end up going through if you paste the markdown into Pandoc directly:
http://pandoc.org/MANUAL.html#processing-each-line
Pandoc's "default" behavior is fine for casual use, but since the

A Dirty Secret About Gotham Parking

In a parking garage around 1 p.m. today, I witnessed
one of the craziest parking maneuvers I've seen in Gotham Parking.
It's a place where people pay for parking at the meter and go for a short
distance to a destination, or perhaps go shopping at the grocery. It would

drive around parked cars so their vehicles won't get a ticket.

A parking attendant, dressed in a gray uniform with navy
blue trim, was trying to direct cars to the far end of the facility, away from
the parking lot.

He was so insistent that I looked to the right and saw
a car parked in front of a fire hydrant to test my car, which had been parked

Wet chemical synthesis of AgNbO3 nanocrystals.
The present work shows that aqueous sodium chloride (NaCl) can act as a complexing agent for the synthesis of AgNbO(3) nanocrystals. First, a series of aqueous solutions containing different mixtures of AgNO(3) and Nb(NO(3))(5) were prepared and the concentration of NaCl employed was varied. The products obtained correspond to pure AgNbO(3) nanocrystals, with crystalline domains of the perovskite structure. It was observed that the best product was obtained with 0.8 molar (M) NaCl. In a second step, a new nanocrystal synthesis was developed using a very simple and novel approach. The key point in this synthesis was the use of a low concentration of NaCl together with a large molar excess of Tween 80, at pH below 8. We found that this combination allowed us to obtain nanocrystals with high morphologies and a very small distribution of size. Finally, the structural properties of the Nb(V) compound were also investigated and were found to be comparable to those of the Nb(IV) compound.

Dream Home - A Story

I dreamed this last night. It went something like this, the beginning at least. I was on a long walk, on the way to a friend I hadn't seen in a while. I was in an orange robe, holding a lantern, and I was walking up a trail that climbed toward the high mountains. The trail and the mountains were dark, with snow on the ground, and there were no signs of human life anywhere near. I knew that, while I can't read, the people I love will send me guidance if needed, so I was trusting they had sent me off to die.

A few hours or maybe days into the hike, I stopped to rest. I took the time to sit down next to a beautiful pond that filled the bottom of a large, dark valley. I was so thirsty by that time, I was about to drink straight from the crystal clear water. I sat down, and noticed I wasn't even close to standing up. A young man in a long, dark robe came up to me. He sat down next to me, and held my hand in his. He said, "You're not trying to die here, are you?" I told him I was. He

An Australian man jailed for having sex with a 14-year-old under the age of consent has been charged with another sex offence.

Sebastian James Green, 42, has been charged with having sex with a 15-year-old girl he met on the internet.

It is understood the girl told authorities Green had taken her virginity and arranged to meet her at his Brisbane home a few months ago.

Green, a resident of Victoria, has since pleaded guilty to the charge.

He was jailed last year after being found guilty by a District Court jury of unlawful sexual intercourse.

He was given a two-year prison sentence along with a two-year community corrections order, which will be served on his release.

Green, who will be released from Parramatta prison on September 22, was also fined $4000, but his lawyers have lodged an appeal against his conviction.

Mortality at one year following elective percutaneous coronary revascularisation in a European cohort of unselected patients.
The rate and causes of early mortality in unselected patients with coronary artery disease who undergo elective percutaneous coronary revascularisation (PCR) in clinical practice is largely unknown. Consecutive patients undergoing PCR at five hospitals in Norway, Sweden, and England were identified using a case-note review and questionnaires. Patients were followed up for 1 year. Mortality was determined by review of hospital medical records. We report early mortality according to whether patients had an angioplasty (including stenting) only or were managed medically. We examined causes of death. Between September 1996 and September 2003, 3,011 of 3,216 (94.4%) patients were included. The procedural rate was 88.4%. Angioplasty only was performed in 1,983 patients (69.3%). The mean age was 66.7 years; 57.3% were male and 69.1% had a known history of coronary artery disease. Mortality according to the presence of significant ST-segment depression was 4.5% following angioplasty only compared to 5.2% following medical management (p = 0.40). Overall, the primary endpoints of death or nonfatal myocardial

Ferrimagnetism of a single Mn3N molecule on graphite.
A single Mn atom is predicted to form a 3D Mn3N molecular network on Cu2N. This was proved by the growth of (6,6) single-molecule magnet Mn3N, which is of interest in physics and chemistry, on graphite. The Mn3N molecule has a (3x)C3-C3 structure. From magnetic measurements, the average Mn-N exchange interaction J is calculated to be -21.4(6) cm-1. The magnetic moment of one Mn atom is calculated to be 4.03, and that of Mn3N is 8.93.

The influence of alexithymia and cognitive appraisal on treatment outcome.
Alexithymia (difficulty identifying and describing emotions) and cognitive appraisal (of illness) may be associated with noncompliance to treatment in patients with coronary artery disease. The goal of this study was to determine the potential mediating role of cognitive appraisal and alexithymia, on the outcome of a psychological stress management treatment, in patients with coronary artery disease. Patients completed the 20-item Toronto Alexithymia Scale and the Beck Depression Inventory at the beginning of the treatment and before the beginning and end of the treatment. Cognitive appraisal was measured at the end of the treatment. Alexithymia correlated with initial depressive symptoms. Alexithymia and depression predicted lower treatment outcome. Although alexithymia predicted no change in cognitive appraisal, cognitive appraisal predicted a decrease in alexithymia at the end of treatment. Alexithymia correlated with increased cognitive appraisal at the end of treatment. When both factors contributed independently to the outcome of the treatment, however, alexithymia was the strongest predictor. Alexithymia and cognitions may be important to consider in the management of cardiac patients in primary care settings.

Often considered to be one of the most powerful and dangerous magical weapons in the world, the Philosopher's Stone allows the transmutation of any element into gold or pure energy, using transmutation magic to create other Philosopher's Stones. The Stone was in the possession of both Hades and Zeus. The Stone is now in the possession of the current king of the Underworld, Aegeus.

The Stone is also the weapon that transmuted Medusa into stone. The only way Medusa was eventually defeated was in the form of a snake.

The philosopher's stone is commonly described as a lump of rock, but it is very clearly shown in the movies as a glowing green stone with two parts that come together. This is very different than traditional mythological depictions of the stone, where it's generally shown as a smooth rock. It can also be a gemstone.

The stone was originally made by the alchemists, but it then vanished somewhere around 400 BCE. A lot of speculation has been made as to where it might be, but it is most commonly believed to have been destroyed when it was in the possession of the alchemist and inventor Archimedes.

The data on the occurrence of fungal strains in the southern area of Primorsky krau and their effect on a plant-parasitic nematode, Heterodera zeae, were studied. A total of 2 species of fungi, Fusarium oxysporum and Phoma exigua, as well as the nematophagous fungus Ampelomyces quisqualis were found in the study area. The study material was collected from the soil and the roots of winter wheat, Festuca arundinacea, and from the surface of wheat seeds in different vegetation zones. Heterodera zeae was isolated from the roots and seeds of winter wheat and Festuca arundinacea, and from the leaf surface of Phragmites cuspidatus. The influence of fungi on the survival, growth, and reproduction (fruiting) of the nematode and the effects of the nematode on the plants were studied. The invention relates to a device for the detection of magnetic field strength, comprising an electronic unit which contains a coil system disposed in the magnetic field, the coil system comprising primary and secondary coils interconnected in a star configuration, and the electronic unit is disposed in a housing having at least one opening for the discharge of air from the interior of the housing.
Such a device is used to detect magnetic fields. A magnet may be scanned when the device determines the intensity and/or direction of the magnetic field that it experiences. The detection of magnetic fields by magnetometers is used to determine the orientation of a permanent magnet or a non-permanent magnet and to detect the magnitude of magnetic fields.
Devices for the detection of magnetic fields are used, in particular, in the magnetic-track-drive systems of magnetic disc drives, for example. They serve, particularly in the case of magnetic heads for magnetic-track-recovery systems, to determine the distance of the magnetic head from the magnetic disc and to align the magnetic poles of the magnetic head perpendicularly to the magnetic disc. Thus, the magnetic field to which the magnetic head is exposed is in a known direction; on the other hand, the magnetic field at the location of the magnetic head is variable.

[Surgical treatment of post-traumatic dislocation of the sternoclavicular luxation].
A method of treatment of dislocation of the clavicle from the sternum following fracture-dislocation of the sternospes is described. This was performed in 15 patients; dislocation was corrected in all of them. The results were satisfactory in 90-95 percent of the patients.

The Government will also step up publicity on the legal and taxation implications related to same-sex marriages, said the Minister.

"The Government will make sure the necessary legal and taxation provisions are in place in 2014. These will take into account the need to ensure a fair system which will be accepted and befit the Singapore community."

Singaporeans should not allow their personal feelings towards same-sex marriage to determine the outcome of the debate on such a contentious issue, Goh reminded Singaporeans.

Singapore's laws are not the best, but they are good enough for the vast majority of Singaporeans to accept.

They are good enough for every citizen to be able to find work and to stay here permanently.

It is for this reason that the Government has a duty to uphold the common sense of a tolerant and open society.

Tumor-associated macrophage-induced angiogenesis in human squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck: the involvement of vascular endothelial growth factor.
Squamous cell carcinomas of the head and neck (SCCHN) are associated with a rich inflammatory infiltrate of macrophages. Tumor-associated macrophages (TAMs) are one of the most abundant leukocyte types in SCCHN, express a variety of proteins that contribute to cellular interactions and, in some types of tumors, promote tumor angiogenesis. We investigated the possible involvement of TAMs in tumor-induced angiogenesis in SCCHN and the possible role of vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) in this process. Immunohistology was performed in primary SCCHN of the head and neck region to investigate the role of TAMs and VEGF in tumor-induced angiogenesis. The expression of TAM antigens, M2 and M1 markers, and VEGF were analyzed to establish whether or not TAMs and VEGF contributed to tumor-induced angiogenesis. TAMs infiltrated tumor nests in almost all (96.9%) of the examined primary SCCHN of the head and neck region, and the VEGF expression in these tumors ranged between 0.20 and 100%. TAMs were found to stimulate angiogenesis in the tumors (P < .01).

Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) is a devastating neurological illness that causes the degeneration of both upper and lower motor neurons. Despite the fact that ALS is an inherited disease caused by genetic mutations in both the ubiquitinated and non- ubiquitinated proteins, the causes for the initiation of the disease and the role of ubiquitination as an initiator for the motor neuron destruction are not understood. We have obtained exciting new data suggesting that ubiquitinated proteins associate with and activate the receptor tyrosine kinase MET that is highly expressed in a subset of motor neurons. The activation of MET results in downstream signaling pathways that are involved in excitotoxicity and the degeneration of motor neurons. While the activation of MET is important for the disease process, ubiquitinated proteins and MET appear to be only partially involved. We and others have identified mutations in the ubiquitinated proteins that may be the initiator for the activation of MET. Since mutations in both ubiquitinated and non-ubiquitinated proteins are found in about 20% of familial and 5% of sporadic ALS cases, it is likely that additional proteins such as the receptor tyrosine kinases are involved in the initiation of the disease and that ubiquitination plays a modulatory role, rather than the initiating role, for all cases of ALS. [Changes in the lipid content of the liver tissue of rats with acute cholestasis].
The hepatic lipid metabolism was studied in acute cholestasis in intact and hypophysectomized rats and in rats receiving hydrocortisone acetate. Liver tissue was shown to contain 3.5 times more cholesterol esters and 25 times less phospholipids than in intact animals. The activity of fattyacid synthase (FAS) was found to increase in intact and hypophysectomized rats on the background of cholestasis. The liver tissue of hydrocortisone-treated rats demonstrated a decrease in the FAS activity and a rise in the activity of the cholesterol ester transferase (the key enzyme catalyzing the esterification of cholesterol) and in the phospholipid content during cholestasis. Possible mechanisms of hepatosis development associated with cholestasis, as well as the functional role of hypophysectomy in the pathogenesis of cholestasis were discussed.

When James Rouse heard about an effort to get the state to allow him to build in Prince George’s County, Md., a giant glassy complex of condos, a hotel, retail and a movie theater, Mr. Rouse had a question: Why would anyone want to live there?

The county’s median household income was $54,000 in 2016, according to census data. A quarter of the homes — and two-thirds of the vacant land — in the county were rentals or “not owner occupied,” meaning that property was owned by someone other than the householders, according to the County’s website.

The median sale price for a home in Prince George’s was $230,739 in 2016, the latest reported county data. A luxury apartment complex for renters like Mr. Rouse’s $1.5 billion project, called Crosswater, was one of four projects the county was considering for the Cove Point site, according to county planning records.

Hemodynamic effects of the new calcium antagonist, nimodipine, in man.
Nimodipine (Bay e 9736) is an effective calcium antagonist. The hemodynamic and biochemical effects of an intravenous dose of 20 mg in healthy human subjects were studied using invasive hemodynamic and noninvasive biophysical techniques. Nimodipine dose dependently lowered both systemic arterial blood pressure and cardiac output without producing systemic vasodilation, consistent with alpha-adrenergic-blocking activity. Total peripheral vascular resistance fell, as did blood pressure in the internal carotid arteries. Although systemic capillary pressure fell, no significant change in total forearm blood flow (FFB) occurred. The plasma concentrations of three metabolites of nimodipine were increased over 300% after 30 min. We performed percutaneous coronary intervention for coronary in-stent restenosis in 10 consecutive patients (12 lesions), using a specially designed balloon-expandable, nitinol microcatheter. The lesion length ranged from 3 to 11.8 mm (median 7.25), the minimal luminal diameter ranged from .2 to 1.6 mm (median 0.5), and the in-stent/in-plaque diameter ranged from 0 to 1.0 mm (median 0.75). [Neutropenia in a patient with myeloma. Apropos of a case report].
Clinical and myelogram features of a myeloma patient are described in the absence of osteolytic bone involvement and hypercalcemia. This is an exceptional feature of the disease, though more frequently described at an advanced stage of the myeloma, with bone destructive lesions. The myelogram is typical in this patient with presence of an atypical plasmocytic picture, and hypercellular bone marrow infiltration with light chain presence in most of the cells. An increased number of peripheral neutrophils and lymphocytes is described. The treatment was based on melphalan and prednisone, with a response without improvement of the clinical and myelogram picture. This is the first description of a case of myeloma with no evidence of bone lesions and the presence of atypical myelogram. Finally, it is reported the presence of an increased number of neutrophils and lymphocytes, a finding not described previously in the literature.

As electronic information becomes ubiquitous, so does the need to process that information securely. This need is manifest in the computer and communications industry as companies seek to transmit private messages through the Internet and other electronic communication mediums over open networks.
A number of technologies have been developed to meet this need. For example, some technologies use an encryption algorithm to encode private messages. Encryption techniques transform an electronic message to render it unreadable by unauthorized parties, even if they gain access to the message. Encryption technologies are available in both software and hardware.
Other technologies that prevent unauthorized disclosure of electronic messages also rely on encryption to transform private messages into unreadable form before sending them across a network. The message is transmitted over an encrypted channel and decoded at the receiving end. These systems use cryptologic keys, which must be communicated over the network, to encrypt and decrypt the messages.
For these and other reasons, secure electronic communications over open networks have become commonplace.

BANGKOK — They say it doesn’t pay to advertise in Bangkok.

Ads for the ubiquitous noodle-soup restaurants, massage parlors and bars are everywhere, plastered on walls, placed prominently in newspapers, placed in shops all over so there are little signs or small printed notices saying “free advertising!” It is difficult to take such an offer seriously.

But it is more difficult still to take the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration’s suggestion seriously that there is no public money for an advertising campaign to promote the city and encourage people to come here.

That the campaign, called “Discover Bangkok,” will be paid for by a special tourist tax, or that the money will come from the national tourism fund run by the Tourism Ministry, are among the many contradictions that could have been avoided if the Bangkok tourist office had simply followed good advice and advertised itself.

“It’s obvious that it is advertising,” said Somchai Wongsai, the deputy director general of the tourism bureau. “Advertising is a part of the promotion of the city. It’s not just about promotion, it’s about advertising. We’re advertising.”

The approach is based on the recently discovered connection between the density matrix (DM) framework of open quantum systems and the standard input-output theory (INFT). These findings were recently proposed in this journal for the description of quantum optics without assumptions about the spectral decomposition of the Hamiltonian in terms of eigenstates of a photon number operator [@JOSAB]. Tobacco, alcohol and cardiovascular disease: a review.
While there is ample evidence that a smoker has an increased risk of coronary artery disease, it is not clear that this greater risk is the result of a direct effect of tobacco on the development of atheroma. Smoking and drinking, in contrast to a single factor, act collectively on the formation of atheroma and are associated with enhanced coronary risks. The effects of these agents depend on the rate and nature of their use. In addition, factors which may reduce the risk of disease development, such as the use of aspirin and beta-blockers, may themselves modify the relation between cardiovascular risk due to alcohol or tobacco and the development of atheroma. Furthermore, it is possible that the effect of both of these substances is confounded by the effects of other behaviours. These include obesity, hypertension and possibly increased cholesterol levels. The effects of these other factors on the development of atheroma, however, are less well defined. The effects of different patterns of alcohol consumption and of variations in the alcohol-containing beverages consumed are likewise unclear. The interaction between tobacco and alcohol is an area of considerable research activity but it is unclear at present which specific behaviour is responsible for the observed increased risks.

Dale Steyn is back, and if the Proteas’ captain and most experienced bowler can deliver at the World Cup on his home turf, the chances of them reaching the semi-finals for the second time in as many campaigns may be high.

South Africa has had to rely on a collection of new faces in order to remain in the hunt for the tournament, but there is a genuine belief that they can cause some upsets in all the right places.

The match, played at Old Trafford, is in the history books but it is a clash of styles and skills that South Africa will be keen to take advantage.

Titanic: a man-made disaster

By Stephen McKevitt (c) 2008, HarperCollins Publishers Ltd.

The Titanic was Britain’s most infamous catastrophe, its loss regarded as the nation’s greatest sea disaster of all time, and for many it was the ultimate symbol of the country’s decadence and moral failure. A former journalist, Stephen McKevitt is in little danger of overdoing the melodrama. The Titanic did indeed destroy and kill and left hundreds of bodies floating in the North Atlantic, and its own story is an extraordinary tale of disaster on the high seas. But what makes the book such a riveting read is its emphasis throughout on the impact of the Titanic on all those it touched on board and shore – from the British to the German and Russian.

When the Titanic sank on 15 April 1912, more than 900 people perished, far more than the number of British and Americans who died in all of World War II. 1. Field of the Invention
Sickle cell anemia is a hereditary disease that manifests itself clinically as an abnormality in erythrocytes by a combination of abnormal hemoglobin (Hb) molecule and abnormal red blood cell (RBC). In the presence of partial oxygen and other ligands, the abnormal RBC deforms and become sticky with other cells, making their presence recognizable. The stickiness of the cells with each other and RBC clots leads to a progressive obstruction in smaller and smaller blood vessels.
The condition of sickle cell anemia has plagued mankind throughout its history. In its acute manifestations, this disease has been known to affect people of many racial groups worldwide. The frequency in which it appears differs from population to population around the world. In sub-Saharan Africa, sickle cell disease occurs in about 1 out of every 2000 births.
It has also been known from experimental data that some sickle cell disease patients have a relative resistance to malaria. In those patients it has been observed that they are able to effectively clear the parasite or are at least able to limit the parasite growth to a lesser extent than normally seen in the average patient.
The use of hydroxyurea is well documented in therapy for sickle cell disease and other genetic disorders of the erythrocytes. Hydroxyurea

A couple years ago, the idea of “gamifying” work was pretty novel. Now, many companies use games as part of their corporate culture – whether it’s building a brand through in-game promotions or just keeping employees entertained – companies such as Facebook and Amazon have implemented daily in-house tournaments, and even some colleges are incorporating gaming into their programs for students. But, as is often the case, it starts within the company and builds out from there.

For game-centric corporate cultures, one of their challenges is how to properly motivate non-gamers to join the gaming sessions because of how much time (sometimes hours) it would take for people to acclimate to. That is until they came up with a clever solution, known as “Skee-Ball.”

Skee-Ball is a simple game using a ball that can be moved around the table to roll in one direction for a point, but moves backwards to score negative points. When the ball rolls into the hole at the center and stays there for two rolls, it is given free Skee-Ball. Because of how simple the game is, each player can take a turn to move the ball and it’s completely impartial. The rules also allow for multiple balls

1. Technical Field
This invention relates in general to computer systems and in particular to controlling the temperature of computer components within computer systems. Still more particularly, this invention relates to a method and system that may be used to automatically cool and control the temperature within computer components in a computer system.
2. Background Art
Computer systems, such as notebook personal computers or notebooks, comprise various internal components that generate heat during periods when the computer is operational. These components are typically mounted within housings. Heat produced by the internal components may cause these components to fail or to operate at temperatures that may cause a user of the computer to experience discomfort. Therefore, a user may be required to periodically manually place the computer in a “standby”, “sleep”, or other mode of non-operation to reduce the temperature of the components prior to placing the computer in an operational mode. In situations where the computer is placed in an operational mode without cooling, the internal components must compensate for the additional heat produced during an operational period.
Manually placing cooling fans and other cooling equipment into an active or non-idle state has been shown to be the safest and most effective solution. The prior art teaches using an integrated computer control system that turns off the cooling system during a user

Innovation in the pharmaceutical industry: what is it?
This review summarises work undertaken by the IFPMA in response to various reports and consultations conducted by the European Commission, which have focused on the theme of industrial innovation in the pharmaceutical sector. The first area addressed is that of what is meant by 'manufacturing innovation' in the context of Europe's innovation policy. The IFPMA's 'Manufacturing Innovation' concept has been developed to address the need to move away from a focus on the physical processes of R&D to take a broader view of the whole process of innovation. An evaluation of the pharmaceutical sector reveals that manufacturing innovation has not yet become a mainstream concern of policymakers, academia and industrial R&D departments, and this situation is partly due to an apparent lack of terminology. The IFPMA therefore calls for a terminology to be agreed that is relevant to industry and academia, and to be used to provide definitional clarity regarding the pharmaceutical sector as a whole, with a view to stimulating discussion and debate on these important issues. The second area addressed concerns the need to align industrial R&D with the European Innovation Scoreboard, and the third is that of the role of EU-funded research within industry. We are all going to die

One of the most memorable moments of my life was watching the death of an ailing friend who was undergoing a complicated surgery for a herniated vertebrae. His heart stopped as surgeons tried desperately to resuscitate him. We, who loved him, could not stop the procedure to intervene.

In my entire clinical experience, I had never witnessed such a scene. I wondered at that point how humans survived such a thing. The next time I took an elective student to see a patient, I took the student to watch another friend die. I did not want him to go through this as my student.

It is interesting that the first step we all must take in learning to work with patients is to realize that we are all going to die someday. When we realize this reality, it becomes easier for us to see our patients as a whole in their totality and not just an individual. A patient may have terminal cancer, the last surgery may end up being amputation, a patient may be HIV positive, but he also may be a wonderful loving uncle, who can be relied upon for many things. A simple method for the determination of sulfite and sulfitolysis products from the reaction of sulfite with fucose.
A simple method for the determination of sulfite and its reaction products from the reaction of fucose with sulfite at neutral pH is described. The reaction is stopped by mixing with a large excess of acetic acid. The reaction products are separated by adsorption on a strong anion-exchange resin containing 5% (w/w) triethylammonium chloride (DEAE). Sulfite reacts slowly with fucose to form an adduct. The reaction of fucose with sulfite is not affected by the presence of acetaldehyde. However, acetaldehyde reacts slowly with sulfite to form an adduct. An addition of acetaldehyde to an acetic acid solution decreases the amount of adducts formed from fucose. The adducts are separated from the remaining solution by chromatography on DEAE and then identified by UV spectrophotometry. The adduct of fucose with sulfite is a dimer and its spectrum is identical to that of the adduct of D-fucose with sulfite. On the other hand, the adduct of fucose with acetaldehyde has a completely different spectrum. The content of total sulfolysis products in fructose is 0.9-2.7

A recent article on the Cato Institute’s website is entitled The Myth of a ‘Public Option’: Why the ‘Public Option’ Would Be an Expensive Tax Hike. It is based on an opinion piece in the Wall Street Journalby the author and editor in chief of the Journal, Robert J. Samuelson.

The Cato article was one of several pieces by conservative commentators on the “public option” health insurance. Like other opponents, Samuelson and Co. make a lot of dubious arguments but their most basic objection is the same: it would cost a lot of money, lots of money, to set up and run a “public option.”

The Cato article is a variation on a similar argument. It states:

Public officials are fond of saying “public option” health insurance is the answer to our health-care woes. But this is only part of the truth. Answering the basic question – what can and should government do – will require a long list of choices. How do we get the most people covered? What’s the best way to pay? How do we make it work fairly? What are the trade-offs? What if “the government” spends a lot of money?

Fungal infection is one of the most common etiological factors for skin disorders, particularly in immuno-compromised patients ([@b0165]). In immunocompetent hosts, superficial fungal infections are treated easily with topical application of antifungal therapeutics in mild cases. Nonetheless, in immunocompromised patients, including individuals with human immunodeficiency virus or solid organ transplant recipients, systemic fungal infection may ensue and may be life-threatening ([@b0005]). Antifungal chemotherapies to treat systemic mycoses have had limited success in these patients ([@b0110]), in contrast to a number of available antileishmania drugs ([@b0045]). As a result, the search for new antifungal agents continues ([@b0035], [@b0200], [@b0070]).

The *Candida* genus is the etiologic agent for candidiasis in both medical and nonmedical patients ([@b0190]), with an increasing trend toward resistance to available antifungal therapies ([@b0015], [@b0145]). *C. albicans* is the most common species involved in systemic infection ([@b0140]). The Best Ways to Protect Yourself from Identity Theft

Identity theft is very serious business with millions of people affected and thousands becoming victims each year. Identity theft occurs when someone uses your personal information to obtain credit cards, mortgages, bank loans, or even a job.

Identity theft is not just an embarrassment-it can be very costly.

If someone steals your social security number or credit card number, they have the potential of obtaining almost unlimited amounts of credit, without your personal knowledge. For example, a fraudster could purchase a $100,000 credit card using a stolen social security or credit card number.

There are many types of identity theft. Here are some of the different types of identity theft:

Fraud: an intentional act of deception by someone who has stolen your identity. This would include identity theft and embezzling and includes both theft and counterfeit credit use.

Forgery: an intentional act of deception by someone or some business entity to falsely represent an identity.

Lost, destroyed or stolen identity documents: when a identity document is lost, stolen, or destroyed. A lost license, lost credit card or ID is a form of identity theft.

A new method of determining regional fat distribution for the treatment of obesity.
Various methods to measure regional fat distribution for the treatment of obesity have been developed, but all have shown various limitations. The present study aimed to develop a novel method to measure the distribution of visceral adipose tissue (VAT), subcutaneous adipose tissue (SAT), and body fat mass. The study enrolled 50 healthy Japanese women with a normal body mass index (BMI). Total fat mass (TFM), trunk fat mass (TFM), and abdominal fat mass (AFM) from dual energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA), body fat mass (BFM), and visceral adipose tissue (VAT) from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), abdominal SAT, and subcutaneous adipose tissue (SAT) were measured. The protein-tyrosine kinase inhibitor, K-252c, inhibits growth factor-stimulated MAP kinase (extracellular signal-regulated kinase; ERK1) activation and transcriptional activation in fibroblast cells. We have proposed that K-252c inhibits the signaling function of ErbB2 in fibroblasts by competing with epidermal growth factor (EGF) for the interaction of the catalytic domain of the receptor tyrosine kinase with the SH2 domain of the growth factor receptor adapter protein, Shc. Here, we further investigate the ability of K-252c to inhibit signaling in a panel of fibroblast cell lines, each expressing ErbB2 (ERBB2) as either wild type or oncogenic mutants. We demonstrate, in addition, that the signaling inhibition by K-252c does not involve the SH2 domain of Shc but relies on direct binding to the catalytic domain of ErbB2. The first reports of human cases of H1N1 influenza virus were in March 2009, and H1N1 has spread worldwide. A total of 1.3 million cases of 2009 H1N1 cases were confirmed in Mexico and the USA \[[@B1]\].

Although H1N1 influenza virus causes a generally milder illness than seasonal influenza viruses, several fatal cases have been reported. The clinical courses of H1N1 influenza virus infections are varied and the pathogenesis is still not well understood. Some viral factors of H1N1 influenza viruses, such as viral polymerase activities, and host cell-related factors, such as the cytokine response, may influence the clinical course of the infection \[[@B2]\].

Human respiratory viruses (including seasonal influenza viruses, human rhinoviruses, respiratory syncytial virus, coronaviruses, and the human metapneumovirus) are responsible for about 15%--25% of exacerbating exacerbations of COPD \[[@B3]\] and 50%--70% of acute exacerbations \[[@B4]\]. Viral or bacterial infections are often coexistent with COPD, and exacerbations of COPD are often caused by superinfections with these respiratory viruses or bacteria \[[@B5]\].

[Phenotypic plasticity of a lizard with different habitat specialization].
As a first step toward understanding the role of different morphological traits in the environmental niche differentiation, we studied allometry, ontogeny, and plasticity of different morphometric body traits in a lizard species occupying different habitats. We found that there are great differences between habitats the same lizards use for thermoregulation, thus indicating a large morphological plastic variability of this species. These differences were revealed in all traits studied. The same character in a lizard from the warm habitat of southern steppe differed markedly from it in a lizard from the cold climate of northern steppes. At maturity, all the traits studied were different. We established that some of the traits were less plastic than others. However, the same trait in a lizard from the warm habitat and a lizard from the cold habitat had lower plasticity than in a lizard from the steppe. The greatest difference in the degree of plastic adaptation was revealed in body size, with the habitat being an important factor determining the variability. In the steppe, the body size was higher than in other habitats.

Radiotherapy in advanced prostatic cancer.
This report concerns the treatment of 25 patients with prostatic cancer (15 with metastases, 10 without metastases). Patients younger than 65 years old were treated by a program of hormonal castration together with radiotherapy. Patients over 65 years old were treated by castration only and by radiotherapy if metastases were present. The radiotherapy was administered in the conventional fractionated dosage of 2.000 rad in the course of 4 weeks. It was then repeated every 6 weeks for a total of 10 fractions. The overall effect was complete (9) or partial (16) response. The patients with hormonal castration and combined treatment obtained a higher response rate. The incidence of side effects was not great.

Mumbai: State Bank of India (SBI) has slashed its interest rate on home loans by up to 2%.

The central bank on Tuesday said it lowered the rates on marginal cost home loans by up to 2% for the first time since 2008.

The new base rate of 4.7% would be available on home loans of up to Rs25 lakh, as per norms of the Reserve Bank to revive demand for these loans.

The new rate would be linked to the MCLR of the marginal cost of funds based on the cost of deposits on a month-end basis.

Also this rate would be available for floating and book savings.

An MCB-branded product will also be brought out under the new rate.

This is in addition to the existing product called the SBI home loan, which is offered at 4.68% interest rate.

Q:

How do I keep my child from throwing temper tantrums?

My one year old just had a full blown screaming and crying tantrum over a toy she really wanted. She's always been fairly easy going but this is the first time I've ever seen her so out of control. I'd love some suggestions on what I could've done differently.

A:

The first thing to do in any circumstance where an adult loses control of their behavior is to stay calm, explain calmly that you're sorry and are doing the best you can to work through the situation and how they should be behaving. This should be done immediately in as calm a tone as possible. The reason for this is because if the child perceives their parent as getting upset, the child will perceive the situation as potentially dangerous (though in this case, you may find yourself in the position of trying to protect the toy from the child; they're usually not actually hurt by their toddler self, but rather are hurting themselves by not listening to you). 
In the meantime, calmly give the child something else that does interest them and will make a noise or have some movement or something that will distract from the item they're currently interested in. Be sure to praise the

A high-frequency generator of the magnetron type using the electron cyclotron resonance effect has been developed at various times by the present applicant. Cytoplasm and membrane composition of the human erythrocyte infected with Plasmodium falciparum: a scanning and transmission electron microscopical study.
Changes in the shape, size, cytoplasmic and perifungal plasma membrane of erythrocytes infected with Plasmodium falciparum and its relationship to the development of parasitemia were studied by scanning and transmission electron microscopy. As the numbers of parasites become large, the infected erythrocytes tend to become spherical, as well as to lose their normal discoid biconcave shape and to assume a sickle-like form. At a time when the intracellular multiplication of the parasites is at its maximum (up to 15-20 parasites/cell), the erythrocyte becomes a very large cell (up to 13,500 A3) occupying almost the entire volume of the infected host cell. Large numbers of the infected cells were found to have lost their functional membrane (plasma and/or vesicular) domains, thus resulting in only the protoplasmic portion of the cytoplasm remaining within the cell membrane. A:

There is no "state" of your repository on github. Each repository has its own state that you should keep in sync yourself. You will have to push/pull the changes from your local master.
To make it more simple, the workflow that works in your case is:

Fetch all changes from remote and update your local repository from origin.
Update only the files you really need to update

A new study is looking at the relationship between sleep, weight loss — and your ability to stay motivated. This one’s focused on college students, looking at the effect of sleep deprivation on their ability to lose weight. In a nutshell, it shows that sleep-deprived college students — particularly girls — lose more weight when they stay at a healthy weight than if they’re under-sleeping and trying to lose weight. The results of an investigation that was presented at the annual meeting of the Associated Professional Sleep Societies, a professional group for those in the field.

Researchers at Washington University in St. Louis and The Ohio State University have demonstrated the link between sleep deprivation and weight loss. Researchers asked female students to sleep for four hours the night before a laboratory test that measured metabolism, which is the rate at which cells burn calories through respiration and the production of heat. During the night before the test, volunteers consumed a low-calorie, low-fat diet. They were not allowed to exercise, but were in a controlled environment, with their beds made-up and the temperature carefully controlled. Researchers found that those who lost weight were more sleep-deprived, while those who gained weight were less sleep deprived.

At an operating frequency of the order of 1 Gbit/s, twin cables offered very high signal quality since the signal propagation time was smaller than a minimum pulse, for example 1 ns. Such cables have proven to be particularly suitable for the transmission of digital signals.
However, at frequencies of more than 1 Gbit/s, signal quality generally decreases, even though the electrical length of the cables has not changed. Signal propagation over long distances must be bridged by repeaters or the signal cannot be transmitted. Furthermore, the use of twin cables is limited by regulations to a maximum of 500 m.

Q:

Rails 4 Action Controller Error

i just upgraded to rails 4 and i am having an issue with my application throwing this error
    rake aborted!
    NoMethodError: undefined method `stringify_keys' for :string_url:Symbol
    /path/to/project/app/controllers/links_controller.rb:34:in `set_content'

  controllers/links_controller.rb:33:in `set'

link_controller has the following code:
      def set
        @link = Link.new({params: {link_id: @link.id}})
      end

      def set_content
        @link.update_attributes(:content => content)
      end

Any ideas why this error would be thrown as it worked in the rails 3.2 version

A:

You have this line in your controller:
@content = Link.new("string_url" => link)

That is a call to the string_url method on your Link class (which is probably not defined). Change it to string :name => link and the error should go away. A Ruby symbol is essentially a string

The first description of coronary artery aneurysm was reported by William Hunter in 1811 and later by Caleb Bagley in 1852. In most cases the etiology of coronary artery aneurysms is undetermined because of insufficient information derived from coronary artery biopsy in the past, which has limited the development of therapeutic strategies. How to find last file in a directory?

Suppose I have the following two files.

I need to delete f1.csv because it is older than f2.csv. For all the files in my directory, I need to look up the last modified time to determine the last updated time. 
I read up on the date command but do not understand it.
Any help appreciated. 

A:

As of POSIX, there is no standard library function to do this, but many systems and libraries provide such functionality.
A simple solution on GNU systems using GNU time (on systems that do not have it, see the manual):
$ echo $(($(date --date=@{} +%s-%N)) % 100)
%H%M%S

The date command can also be used to format the output:
%c   # for the date (same as --date=now)
%T   # for the current time (without timezone suffix)

Another solution is to use stat:
$ stat -c "%n %y" mydir/* 
243588 2016-10
83936 2016-06
227778 2016

A liquid crystal display (LCD) panel is an instrument for displaying image information on a screen. The transmissive LCD is often used by the ordinary consumer, and can be used in a broad range of applications. The backlight device for the transmissive LCD can be a Light Emitting Diode (LED) backlight device, a Cold Cathode Fluorescent Lamp (CCFL) backlight device, or a Hot Cathode Fluorescent Lamp (HCFL) backlight device.

Cocaine abuse has become a major health problem worldwide. Although the initial use of "street" drugs such as cocaine involves direct injection, there is considerable evidence suggesting that cocaine and many other drugs of abuse produce both their acute reinforcing effects and their rewarding effects through effects on multiple brain regions. The overall objective of this project is to determine how stimulant and opiate drugs of abuse interact with the brain to produce their different behavioral actions. In the initial stages of the proposal, we will determine to what extent the behavioral actions of cocaine and morphine depend on actions in the central amygdala (CeA), since the amygdala appears to play a major role mediating the reward-related effects of opiates as well as stimulant drugs. In parallel studies, we hope to further our understanding of the cellular mechanism underlying the acute effects of various addictive drugs. In studies using in situ hybridization, we have found that opiate receptor agonists (e.g., morphine) increase the expression of dynorphin in many brain areas where opiate receptors are present, including a number of brain regions thought to play a role in mediating opiate reinforcement and dependence. Our recent data indicate that chronic treatment with cocaine can produce large changes in both dynorphin and enkephalin gene expression after acute administration.

abstract: 'We present a numerical study of the structure of the magnetised ICM using the adaptive mesh refinement Tree+SPH code [ENZO]{}. A key result is that all numerical models have very large, unphysical, values of the density near the virial radius. We therefore re-scale the gas density and pressure distributions in the central regions of the models and find that in nearly all cases, the mass within a radius corresponding the X-ray emitting region is only about $1/7$ of the enclosed mass within $r_{200}$. This result implies that X-ray determined total masses, and hence the derived values of the baryon fraction, are much less reliable than is commonly assumed. This is a direct consequence of the high central gas density in the majority of the models that have been presented as reliable X-ray estimates of the masses of clusters and groups of galaxies.'

An American family with severe generalized hypertrichosis with facial involvement.
Three generations of a family presented with generalized hypertrichosis with facial involvement. The propositus, a 46-year-old female from a nonconsanguinous family, presented with diffuse hypertrichosis. She also had thick eyebrows with long roots and thick eyelashes. Her father had generalized hypertrichosis as reported in literature, with unusual involvement of her right cheek including the lips and chin. The mother had eyebrow involvement. The patient had a noncontributory family history. There was no evidence of ectodermal dysplasia, seborrhoeic dermatitis, or acanthosis nigricans. A diagnosis of generalized hypertrichosis with facial involvement was made after a clinical examination, histopathological, and histopathological examination. The diagnosis is discussed.

Synthesis of (1-hydroxyethyl)-N-benzoylalaninamide via the corresponding methyl ester.
Treatment of methyl alpha-D-galacto- or D-glucopyranoside with 2 equivalents of glycine methyl ester, followed by catalytic hydrogenation, provides the methyl O-alpha-(aminoalkyl)-D-xylopyranoside. Oxidation, removal of the protecting group, and treatment with ammonia are the next reaction steps for obtaining the alpha-D-galacto- or the D-glucopyranoside of the corresponding methyl ester N-benzoyl-D-xylo-hexapeptide. The methyl ester obtained is easily activated in a Fischer esterification providing the corresponding acid halide, which, after reaction with N-benzoyl-D-tyrosine methyl ester, results in the N-benzoyl derivative of the appropriate methyl ester of the corresponding alpha-D-galacto- or D-glucopyranoside. Bacterial infections caused by methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) have been increasing worldwide, and their infection-related mortality is high. Moreover, MRSA infection has been increasing in patients that have undergone hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT). Although several antibacterial agents and vaccination strategies against MRSA have been investigated, the clinical efficacy of these approaches has been insufficient. We have previously identified the protective antigen (PA) in S. aureus that induces protection against MRSA infection. The S. aureus surface protein FnBPA, which is a component of bacterial exoskeletons (Exo), was determined to be critical for efficient colonization in the lung and bacteremia in a disseminated infection. In collaboration with other institutes, we investigated the efficacy of recombinant PA (rFPA) immunization to induce a protective immunity against MRSA infection. Administration of rFPA protected mice from lethal challenge with MRSA (Nagase et al, in press). These findings have the possibility of providing a novel and efficient way to control MRSA infections and/or bacteremia in HSCT patients. Human adenoviruses (HAdV) are non-enveloped, linear double-stranded DNA viruses whose tropism has traditionally been limited almost exclusively to the respiratory tract, but which have since been observed as causative agents of systemic disease, including hemorrhagic cystitis, myositis, myocarditis, pancreatitis, and peritonitis ([@B1], [@B2]). Within the past decade, the emergence of more-sophisticated clinical and molecular techniques has enabled HAdV to be identified as frequently complicating both the clinical course and the molecular etiology of various forms of cancer, including those of the hematopoietic system, brain, lung, ovary, and prostate ([@B3]--[@B11]). The mechanisms through which HAdV may directly contribute to tumor development are unknown.

Over the last two decades, a wealth of studies have supported an important role for HAdVs in tumor immunopathogenesis, most often in conjunction with co-infecting tumor viruses, in particular, Epstein--Barr virus (EBV) and human herpesvirus (HHV)-8 ([@B10], [@B12]--[@B16]). In the last years of WW II, many Polish women who volunteered to serve as “angels of mercy” to help liberate Nazi concentration camps began falling in love with Nazi German soldiers, only to regret their actions upon their liberation when told the stories of the atrocities they had committed.

The phenomenon is not unique to Poland (or other countries whose women served as volunteers) — it has also been observed among American women who served in World War II.

In a recently published paper in the American Journal of Sociology, Dr. David G. Lavin of The George Washington University examined some of the stories of women who volunteered to serve in the U.S. Women’s Army Corps during WW II. The author found that the U.S. media, when reporting on the story of the female volunteer, often failed to provide an accurate description of the women’s experiences. The resulting inaccurate stories contributed to a stereotype of women volunteering to serve as “liberating angels” who would be repaid for their efforts by Nazi atrocities.

In most cases, Lavin found that the women volunteers were neither angels nor in thrall to Nazi war culture, but that their wartime experiences were shaped at least as much by the expectations

Heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning (HVAC) systems are widely used to control the comfort level within enclosed environments, such as residential and commercial structures. Such HVAC systems typically include a furnace, an air circulation blower and an HVAC controller that is used to control the amount of delivered air (or other working fluid) to the various rooms of a structure based on various input signals.
As is well known, a “condensate” is sometimes produced in a home or other structure. Typically, this type of condensate has a milky consistency, having a color close to that of white milk. Most of the time this produced condensate is discarded or used for watering the plants and landscaping. However, under certain conditions, this condensate can become contaminated with undesirable chemicals or even pathogens. When this condensate is disposed of in an outside area, this may constitute a health hazard for persons or animals that come into contact with this produced water.
Furthermore, the cost associated with cleaning this type of produced condensate can be significant, as can the time spent by maintenance personnel to do so. Brief Overview of Causation in English Law

Introduction

Causation is a very important topic in English Law - it is the basis upon which a legal right is awarded, and in order to do that it is necessary to find a defendant's fault. This is a topic that is dealt with in a lot of detail in English case law, but for the purposes of this article a summary will suffice.

In English law a claim against a defendant can only survive if it is a direct cause of the harm which is suffered. This can be very difficult to find in everyday situations, because often it can be difficult for a cause of harm to be ascertained.

One type of claim is often the victim's fault - this may be an individual's fault, but can also be the fault of an organisation. In some instances this may be the fault of the perpetrator of a crime. Another type of claim is the fault of an unknown third party, whereby the victim cannot prove that they suffered from a direct causal fault - for example, where a third party has damaged property as a result of acts of terrorism.

@array.max_by(&:max)

Gardner Collin Taylor (9 November 1832 – 27 March 1911) was a Confederate naval officer during the American Civil War. He was the older brother of Confederate Naval General John D. Pender and brother-in-law and commander of USS Kearsarge.

Biography

Early life and career
Taylor was born in New Orleans, Louisiana to James and Caroline (née Streator) Taylor, both of whom were wealthy and belonged to a prominent Creole family. His father was a New Orleans attorney and planter and one of three brothers who all held high political office in Louisiana. Taylor was educated at Phillips Academy (formerly known as the Andover Theological Seminary), and then at Yale College. His classmates included future Confederate General Pierre G. T. Beauregard and future Louisiana governor John McEnery. He was a graduate of the law school of the University of Louisiana, now called Tulane University.

In early 1853, with two classmates and two professors, he sailed to England to study for the bar. Instead of completing his studies at the Inner Temple, he returned to the United States, where he began a successful law practice in St. Louis.

1. Field of the Invention
The invention relates to a motor, more particularly, to a dual-input-voltage motor.
2. Description of Related Art
Traditional motors all require a single voltage source to operate, and are not suitable for use in a machine tool which uses two voltage sources (Vdc1 and Vdc2). Thus, when using two voltage sources (Vdc1 and Vdc2) in a single machine tool, two motors need to be utilized. There are two different ways to use two motors according to the two voltage sources (Vdc1 and Vdc2) that are used. Referring to FIG. 1, in the first way, two motors, e.g., motor 11 and motor 12, are used to operate the machine tool when the voltage sources Vdc1 and Vdc2 are not in use. Each motor 11 or 12 respectively receives power from each of the two voltage sources Vdc1 and Vdc2. Each motor 11, 12 is capable of rotating and each motor 11, 12 includes a rotatable shaft and a rotor installed inside the shaft.
When the voltage sources Vdc1 and Vdc2 are not in use, each of the two motors 11, 12 respectively stops rotating.

**Abstract** We develop a theory of self-similarity for Hamiltonian systems with a time dependent coupling term or an external magnetic field that are confined to a compact interval in phase space. By means of a normal form technique, we reduce them to a system of ordinary differential equations depending on the similarity parameter of the original system and its eigenfunctions. In this way we recover the well-known self-similarities found before in Hamiltonian systems with time-independent fields, where in general the system has to be nonpolynomial in the similarity parameter. In this paper we extend this theory to the case of a self-similar Hamiltonian system with a time-dependent field or coupling. We show, in agreement with the results known in the cases of time-independent fields and coupling, that the self-similar Hamiltonian system has invariant circles and a set of periodic solutions. However, by computing an explicit value of the constant energy density, we obtain that the radius of the invariant circle is infinite. All this means that the self-similar

All patients completed a 3-day diet record at the beginning and end of the study. Survival analysis was performed with the use of the life table procedure. Twenty-three patients died during the study, 17 on the exclusive enteral diet and six on the normal diet. In vivo efficacy, safety and immunogenicity of a recombinant protein vaccine in rainbow trout.
In this study a truncated version of the gene coding for rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss) recombinant protein (rtTRAP(TM/F)) expressed in Escherichia coli and produced by a plant cell-expression system was evaluated as vaccine in rainbow trout. The immune response elicited by intramuscular injection of the rtTRAP(TM/F) in rainbow trout was evaluated by measuring haemagglutination inhibiting antibody titres. The results obtained showed that the vaccine candidate was immunogenic both in rainbow trout and in whitefish injected subcutaneously or intravenously with rtTRAP(TM/F) at two concentrations (5 or 10 microg/fish). Immunoblot and RT-PCR analyses showed that the vaccine generated antibody responses cross-reactive with rtTRAP(TM/F) from other species and with several viral structural proteins. Finally, when the intramuscular administration of a vaccine dosage between 5 and 20 microg/fish was tested, the results showed that all fish developed specific antibody titres and an effective protection could be achieved at doses as low as 5 microg/fish. These results indicate that the recombinant protein could be used as vaccine in rainbow trout.

A:

As many of you have heard our friend and former colleague Joe Soto has left NPR. He is taking a job as president of the University of Southern California. We are grateful for many aspects of Joe's tenure with the National Public Radio, but are heartbroken by his departure. So in keeping in that spirit, we thought we should have a little fun and give it up for the last time in this space.

And here it is.

NPR is known for bringing great radio to millions and millions of people who don't read or watch the news. How great is that?

A few years back NPR started to post a daily photo in the New York Times of an NPR listener from around the world. A few people in the past had seen some of those pictures and thought they were pretty cool. We thought it would be fun to see if we could get a good one of those pictures in the Times.

So we started to look through our photo archives and pull out the best from years past. Many of the portraits we took were from NPR's overseas missions. The idea was the the best listener photos would be sent to the New York Times for printing.

We started this project before Joe Soto was hired full time.

A number of people have written to us recently asking how we can help a parent who has discovered they have a child who has autism. This is a difficult time: our own three children are all diagnosed with the disorder, and in recent months we’ve had friends and relatives who, through no fault of them, have found out that someone they love has autistic spectrum disorder, or ASD. At the same time, we have an ANA policy specifically about how to deal with autism. However, our experience is that many general ANA members have little or no idea about autism.

Autism is a developmental disorder, which means that it is not a mental illness. In most cases, the autism is present from early childhood, and symptoms start to become apparent around three to four years of age. While there may be some delay in the acquisition of many developmental skills – such as speaking, toilet training, handwriting, and playing with imaginary friends – it is common for people with autism to learn to talk by around the age of two and to develop very independent lives by the age of six.

The proposed research is an evaluation and validation of the effects of the psychotherapy of depression. In a first study, the investigators will compare cognitive therapy, behavioral therapy, and combined cognitive and behavioral (behavioral and cognitive) therapy for the treatment of depressed outpatients. The primary outcome measure will be the Montgomery-Asberg Depression Rating Scale. The patients will be treated by their treating therapist on a long-term basis. The investigators will evaluate potential mediating variables, including age, race, sex, and psychosocial impairment. A subset of patients will also be studied using the technique of cognitive history, in order to determine if a particular pattern of cognitive response may be related to differential therapeutic results. A second study will use a parallel design design (including controls) to study the effects of psychotherapy for depressed inpatients, comparing cognitive therapy, behavioral therapy, and combined, versus a wait-list control group. Bilateral optic papillitis: an unusual condition.
This paper describes an unusual case of bilateral optic papillitis. A 16-year-old girl presented with acute bilateral, painless loss of vision that persisted over a 3-month period. Neurological and rheumatologic investigations were negative, but a cerebrospinal fluid cell count showed a lymphocytic pleocytosis and elevated protein levels. Magnetic resonance imaging showed focal areas of enhancement of the optic nerves. The patient was initially managed with a 5-day course of oral prednisolone, and the condition improved sufficiently to discontinue the steroid medication 3 weeks later. Although optic nerve abnormalities are rare in childhood, they should be considered in adolescent patients presenting with acute unilateral or bilateral optic neuropathy.

Q:

How to make vim run a command on save, instead of on exit?

Is there a way to make vim run a certain command every time I save?

Edit something - Command: [something]   # vim saves the file
Edit something - Command: [something]   # vim exits

I hope the question makes sense and is clear - feel free to ask for clarification! Thanks in advance!

A:

Use BufWritePre that triggers whenever write to that file is attempted 
command! -buffer <buffer> Run_command
setlocal buftype=nofile noswapfile nomodified nobuflisted buftype=nowrite
autocmd BufWritePre <buffer> command Run_command

You might also consider to disable write before.

Cellular interactions in the formation of the chick embryonic vitelline membrane.
The development of the chick embryo vitelline membrane was studied using the fluorescence photobleach recovery (PBR) technique. A population density of the membrane forming cells was determined by counting the nuclei in the yolk sac and extraembryonic regions (cattle horn, allantois, and neural fold). The rate of proliferation of the membrane forming cells was found to increase linearly throughout developmental stages 9-14 (E5-E7). After cell proliferation stopped the cells continued to increase in size over the following 1-3 d. As cells approached the size of a small chick embryo the yolk sac and cattle horn showed very little fluorescence. The fluorescence in the allantois and neural fold regions, however, remained constant until the embryo assumed the shape of a sphere. Later (days E8-14) a gradual increase in fluorescence was observed in the yolk sac and allantois, and there was a sharp, rapid increase in fluorescence in the neural fold region. These results suggest that the cells in the formation of the vitelline membrane do not lose fluorescence until they assume the shape of a sphere and do not reabsorb fluorescece until the embryo assumes

Software applications such as word processors, web browsers, email applications and the like are well known in the art. These applications are typically provided as part of the operating system installed on the computer as an application program of one type or another. To run most applications, the user must first specify the application's appropriate directory or directories, and then the user must specify the location of other files needed to use this application. The process of specifying the location of other files needed to use this application is typically tedious and cumbersome.
Specifying the location of files needed to use an application has typically been performed in two ways. One way to provide a path to a specific location within an application is to provide a direct link, such as is done with the “shortcut key” on Microsoft Windows® operating system and the “command shortcut” on many versions of Macintosh® operating systems. (?<!\/\)\s*[^][|]*(?:[^][/][|]*)+\/\?(?:[^][|]*|\()*)

Q:

How are the functions in the following script called?

I'm trying to find out how the script below gets executed.
script -q -c 'print(len($1))' *

The command "print(len($1))" doesn't help me, since in this function len() has to be called to pass a parameter to it. 
Can anybody explain the above command (or at least give a link to some online documentation of it)?

A:

The command print(len($1)) evaluates to the command print(len(first parameter passed)). 
So, first parameter passed is the shell's $0 variable. This variable contains the full path to the current script.
You can use echo $0 to see this:
#!/bin/bash

script -q -c 'echo $0' *

Output:
./my_script

Note:
script and script -q are Bash builtin commands; you'll not find their manual pages in man. They are documented here: bash(1), [1], this question.

If you want to find a bash function, the best way is to search for it in /usr/

The use of chemical pesticides on fruit farms have been one of the major sources of residues in fruits, and can cause environmental pollution due to their persistence in the environment (Giri et al., [@B19]). As an economical strategy, the application of chemicals for insect control is largely used as a measure to increase productivity (Nayak et al., [@B45]). Chemical insecticides can disrupt growth and development of organisms, and may affect human health as well (Hao et al., [@B24]). However, the use of synthetic chemicals is not a preferable solution for solving the insecticide resistance problem and environmental pollution problems (Senthilkumar et al., [@B59]). Therefore, scientists have long been studying the chemical ecology and chemical ecology of non-target organisms (Dantz, [@B13]; Dantz et al., [@B15]). However, the complex chemical ecology and mechanisms of pesticide-induced behavior of pests have not been fully elucidated (Vijayakumar et al., [@B66]).

The pest control products have been commonly applied to the soil, but not applied directly to the target pests (Dyck et al., [@B17]). To eliminate and

abstract: 'In this paper we show how to construct a large class of explicit two dimensional models of a finite number of particles by using the concept of the finite dimensional approximation. We consider a class of simple models describing the interactions between particles confined inside a domain by a pair potential. When the size of the domain and the interaction strengths between particles tend to infinity, under an appropriate scaling limit, the system converges to a deterministic model for which explicit formulas with an exponential accuracy can be deduced at low computational cost, by the use of a semi-explicit solution of the linear system of the finite many particles. We focus on the case in which the domain is a disk. To solve the linear system, we apply the finite elements method based on spectral collocation methods to the discretized form of the equations on the boundary.'

JOHANNESBURG — The United Nations World Food Program says there is widespread hunger in Swaziland. The agency said Swaziland's food situation deteriorated in September.

"Most of those households have either little or no food," the UN food agency's South Africa branch said in a release. "The country's food security situation deteriorated further in September," it said.

The worst hit areas are the central districts of Hlathikhulu, Nhlangano, Mbilini and Manzini where more than 50 percent of residents do not have enough vegetables or fruit for their daily meals.

Most of the people affected are children, with severe acute malnutrition increasing in some of the worst hit areas.

The UN agency says the country’s food security situation continued to deteriorate in September, with an increasing number of vulnerable households (up to half of those who should be benefiting), mainly women, children and elderly people affected, most of them from low-income households.

The UN's International Fund for Agricultural Development is supporting the Swazi government to assist people in vulnerable situations to become food secure, and to enhance agriculture production and resilience to food insecurity.

[Cytomegalovirus as a cause of encephalitis--a report of three autopsy cases].
Neurological involvement is the most frequently observed sequela of acute infection with cytomegalovirus. Three autopsy cases are reported (two autopsied) with cytomegalovirus encephalitis. Two of these cases were children (aged 2 and 6, respectively) and one patient was middle aged man. Focal encephalitic lesions, seen on the brain roentgenograms, and increased activity of cytomegalovirus were revealed by examination using the paraffin section immunoenzyme method and by culture, respectively. These three cases presented common clinical and neuroradiological findings and laboratory features.

  every time I see someone. (Source)

A:

It means what it does in that particular context; it's not ambiguous.
It can be paraphrased as 'I saw them and it was a first time' or 'I saw them, and the fact that I had never seen them before before made me feel very happy'.
The 'first time I had ever seen them' is ambiguous -- it could mean 'the first time I saw them was a new occasion

A position of the objective lens driving part with respect to the position detecting light beam is detected by a position detecting light detecting unit. The human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) is the etiological agent of adult T-cell leukemia (ATL) and inflammatory cardiomyopathy (CIM). In both, the etiological agent is transmitted among members of families, often among generations, by human lymphocyte factor (HTLV-I, II). HTLV is classified among a family of related viruses. Similar virus families include HTLV-II (murine T-cell leukemia virus) and simian T-cell leukemia virus, which cause AIDS-like diseases in infected primates and humans.

HTLV-1 is the etiological agent of aggressive leukemia called Adult T-cell leukemia/Lymphoma (ATL), associated with hypercalcemia and renal failure, as well as the inflammatory form CIM (or T-CIP). In these conditions, most patients progress to an aggressive chronic phase, with a survival time of months or years [@pone.0036127-Bosque1], [@pone.0036127-Bosque2], [@pone.0036127-McConkey1], [@pone.0036127-Houot1], [@pone.0036127-Houot2].
