The trial will be run by biotech company Bioquark Inc, headquartered at the University of California, San Francisco. It will enroll 60 subjects at 16 sites in the US, Canada, Russia and Poland. The trial is designed as a double-blind, placebo-controlled study. Participants will receive either the vaccine or a placebo, with the dose varying from 100ug to 10mg; subjects will be given injections every eight weeks.

“The potential for this vaccine to prevent HIV infection in both vaginal and rectal sexual encounters would be transformative,” said Dr Charles Arntzen, Director of the Division of Clinical AIDS Research, the USF Health Global Health Institute, who will lead the study. “Vaccination of female sex workers with the Quadrivalent vaccine may be one of the most effective ways to achieve global HIV prevention in any one population of women with the highest risk of HIV exposure. “

The vaccine is known as ‘QB-151’.

The vaccine’s developers believe if the vaccine is used, then fewer people will contract HIV because they’ll be less exposed to other sex worker who have the virus. However, the study is run in a manner where this cannot be guaranteed. The study may also help reduce HIV transmission

These lesions commonly occur in men in the sixth, seventh, and eighth decades of life and involve the sellar floor. They can also develop in the sphenoid, parasellar, suprasellar regions as well as in the intra- and suprasellar cisterna, and rarely in the carotid cisterns or cavernous siphon. The tumor growth patterns are variable and include dural (85%), intradural (8%), and extradural (2%) components. The sellar floor is rarely involved [22, 28–30].

text:
Read the full report and discover why PTC’s solution to a high risk and growing problem

At the center of PTC’s solution is its patented Powerlink® Technology that delivers reliable performance and durability using a single, high-efficiency cable.

“The high voltage cable and the high power cable are actually the same cable but with some of the insulation and shielding removed," states Dr. William Reimann, Senior VP and general manager of Powerlink, “and a new circuit board is added to the cable.

“With our Powerlink Technology, we are able to use this cable for very high power needs for our data center applications.”

Using this concept, the Powerlink cabling is designed to carry the same electric current as the industry’s most robust power and data cables.

“While other power cabling products are able to deliver very high data transfer rates to a single computer, they are unable to offer the same level of power transfer to multiple computers, without exceeding design specifications set by the International Electrotechnical Commission,” states Dr. Reimann.

text:
The madness of the 2012 NBA Draft, from Dion Waiters to Michael Beasley, is well documented. But how did the NBA become the league that so famously ignores age and experience? A few simple words can explain it all.

The league's original collective bargaining agreement (CBA) was signed in 1966 and revised in 1967. For those not keeping track, both agreements were designed to get players in pro uniforms as quickly as possible. Teams had to pay players as soon as they graduated from high school; it was a way to circumvent the college signing rules.

The league wanted to ensure that the best players were in the most competitive, most popular game of the day: basketball.

A couple of minor adjustments to the CBA were made in 1973, with one of the primary differences being an added year of experience required for a player to count toward the league limit. The rationale was that the draft was then a two- or three-day event, and not enough time was allotted to develop young talent.

Players who received a three-year college scholarship, at the time, generally started their rookie seasons no later than their final season in high school. An extra year on a college team, the thinking went, would give the young
prompt_prefix: The madness of the 2012 NBA Draft, from Dion

text: The Institute of Preventive Medicine Environmental and Occupational Health at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill (IPM/UCEH) is a multi-disciplinary research group involved in the study of the effects of toxic chemicals in humans and the environment. We are conducting epidemiology studies designed to examine the relation between early life events, such as childhood environment and subsequent occurrence and/or progression of disease in adulthood, on the one hand, and on the occurrence of cancer, specifically breast cancer, on the other. In addition, we are collecting data on the occurrence of cancer in relation to exposure to various environmental exposures as children. We use a variety of methods to collect our information including interviews, questionnaires, medical records, and pathology reports; but primarily from death certification data supplied by the North Carolina State Health Department (formerly the North Carolina Vital Statistics System). The North Carolina death certificate is accepted by the National Center for Health Statistics and is one of only six States that have participated in this program. Death certificate data include the name of deceased, age, gender, date of death, and county, State and U.S. census geography codes; and, in addition, a medical history section which can be used to collect data related to various medical conditions, smoking history, reproductive history

There is also the issue of "copyright misuse", which means that it is a defense in a copyright suit that it is necessary for the copyright owner's lawful use of the copyrighted work; this can be seen as a corollary of the idea of fair use. Here you will find solutions for all your mobile power problems. This includes charging and recharging and protection.

A great battery with a very low capacity should be charged slowly but if you charge it fast the capacity will be low. A good battery with a medium capacity should be charged slowly. If you charge them fast the capacity will get smaller. A great battery should be charged quickly.

Battery life

Battery life is measured in minutes/hours, in the case of some applications it’s reported as ‘kilometers’ etc.

Battery life = Charge time x Capacity

Charge time = (Capacity x Current in Watts) / 1000

When driving with a device the battery is mainly powered by the device’s built in charger. The device usually runs down the battery when on standby. If you never use the device when turned on battery life is high, but if you use the device every day the battery life will decrease.

Battery protection

Battery protection should be taken very seriously. Battery protection is a crucial factor of battery life. It’s important that charging and discharging is properly managed.

text:
I found an interesting article here.
The article goes into how to make all the classes in .Net that extend
Collection generic.
How does this work? Say you have the following class:
public class CustomCollection<T> : Collection<T>,
IEnumerable<T>
{

}

How does the generics definition specify a type argument?
The article is well written but I can't quite understand the mechanics of the whole thing.

A:

Because all these are compile-time type arguments, they're specified in the generic type argument list, not the constructor or the class:
new List<int>    // List<int>
new List<string> // List<string>
new List      // List<T>

The same goes with the classes in question:
new CustomCollection<int>   // CustomCollection<int>
new CustomCollection<string> // CustomCollection<string>
new CustomCollection          // CustomCollection<T>

This works the same for classes that implement interfaces:
new MyObject          // MyObject
new MyInterface<string> // MyInterface<string>

Bitcoin is a currency, used to exchange goods and services. In this sense, it is both a payment instrument and a value unit, with a value of 1 BTC. Bitcoins are created, and henceforth can be divided, by individuals, organizations, banks, and merchants.
The value of the amount of a transaction is determined by the parties' agreement. A transaction requires a valid signature of the bitcoin wallet which contains the transaction data, together with the signature(s) of two or more people. The signatures are calculated over the transaction data using Cryptography. The bitcoin wallet can be the holder's or a third-party's.

The value of 1 Bitcoin is created by supply, and demand, but is fixed in both cases. It is not in the control of any central entity, bank, or entity of government, although it can be controlled by the holder. Like most other value units, there is significant transactional overhead for producing or acquiring bitcoins. Due to this overhead a small amount of bitcoin can be exchanged for a very large amount of fiat currency.

by Garth Fagan

The following piece was written by Garth Fagan in late 1999 as a part of the Utopian Futures project (which took shape as part of the "Utopia Now" exhibition that same year). The piece is a response to the political impasse that characterized the final period of the 1990s, when the world had a tendency to get increasingly disillusioned about the possibility of achieving genuine change.
Since it was published, my views have further solidified in relation to the Utopian and Political dimensions of radical alternatives. I still believe that the latter are, and will always be, vital components of any viable future.

In the last decade alone the global landscape has been reconfigured from a static world divided into nation states, based on the precepts of the 'Western Civilization', into a transnational order where the West has not been the main actor.

The world has witnessed not only major changes in social relations, but also the emergence of new types of actors to promote peace and justice, and to challenge the old ideologies. The 'war on terrorism', which developed as a reaction to the 'war

Husenbeth: Bike Routing Algorithm. With Maté Perrineel. In Proceedings of the 7th Annual International on Transportation Research (ITR'06), 2006.

"This paper presents the first algorithm for bike routing which is scalable to large networks and which uses a time-efficient, non-greedy, cost-aware routing policy. Our algorithm optimally balances the trade off between a high fraction of the available demand covered and a low network distance. We prove, that the expected path length is asymptotically minimized for our algorithm. Experimental evaluation shows that it finds tours with very high demand covered in both short and large networks on average significantly faster than both greedy and non-greedy alternatives on our test cases. Furthermore, our algorithm is particularly suitable for network design with the objective of cost minimization. We compare the run time efficiency and the quality of our optimal and greedy solutions which show that the greedy solution is sometimes not able to find high quality solutions."

The "Bushman" is the aboriginal name for a person who wanders the land hunting game and collecting fruit, berries and nuts.
The traditional food that the Bushmen would carry in their belt pouches is called "mielie meal" and is made from the ground seeds of the Mielie-grass.
To make the millet, you must soak the ground seeds in water overnight. The next day you add some water to this and then spread it out to dry in the sun. After this it is ground.
This millet is then mixed with water to make "mielie meal".
You will need a strong, heavy stick with a sharp pointed end to dig up and cut the Bushman's miel

Hemocompatation analysis based assay technology of heparin pharmaceutical using fluorescence probes, LIVE & DEAD.
Heparin pharmaceuticals, as therapeutic medicine substances, have been clinically applied worldwide as the "standardized product" in order to prevent thrombotic-embolic phenomenon; however, their hemostatic activity and biological effects have not been fully studied because of the lack of adequate analytical techniques. Here we report the measurement of heparin activity using LIVE&DEAD®, which is the fluorescence indicators for cell viability and membrane permeability. It is expected that the results will contribute to better understanding of the clinical use of heparin pharmaceuticals and the development of safer synthetic heparin pharmaceuticals, in addition, the results will promote the development of a novel cell function test for biopharmaceutical products.

[Lobulating tumors with atypical carcinomalike elements of different differentiation occurring before carcinoma transformation, or as a late complication of squamous cell carcinomas. Apropos of a case].
A case of lobulating tumour, of which the epithelial component consisted of a moderately differentiated, squamous cell carcinoma, and the mesenchymal component of a mixed, undifferentiated sarcoma, is reported. The coexistence of distinct epithelial and spindle-cell elements within the same tumour, as well as a clear separation of their malignant behaviour may constitute a major diagnostic problem. The tumour was interpreted as a low-grade squamous-cell carcinoma arising from a pre-existent benign sebaceous lobule, which was transformed into an undifferentiated sarcoma following long latency. The histological criteria of the carcinomatous transformation of a lobular malignant tumour are described, and the different diagnostic hypotheses are discussed.

[Etiiology factors of cerebral vascular diseases and relationship thereof to risk-mark system of occupational health care in miners].
The review describes etiological and clinical-morphological patterns of cerebral vascular diseases (CVD) and its pathogenesis as related to occupational risk factors (RF) in miners with a particular focus of the relationship of these patterns to the risk-mark system. The prevalence of CID in miners increases with age and reaches maximum at about the age of 50 years. The pathogenesis of CID is associated with multiple RF of the occupational environment (RF-OE): chemical factors due to the environment of mine shafts and the nature of mineral excavation (high hydrogen levels, dust, toxicants); mechanic factors of equipment, including the working position of miner; biological RF of miners' body (the influence of stress, smoking, alcohol consumption and other factors). The results obtained are important for the evaluation of effectiveness of antiemesis programs in the mining industry.

Brain metastases of nonhematoderm origin diagnosed during surgical procedures performed at brain tumors resection, and in a subset of patients during intraoperative cerebral monitoring.
Brain metastases (BM) of nonhematoderm origin are usually found at autopsy; however, they may also be detected during the course of surgery or brain tumors resection. We analyzed our experience with these metastases and we tried to identify risk factors that could potentially help clinicians to anticipate their appearance and to select patients who deserve aggressive management. Fifty-seven patients with intracranial BM of nonhematoderm origin were identified. Fifty-eight BM were found at histopathologic examination of the surgical specimen at a single surgical procedure (n=55, 94.8%), at an excision of tumor at a previous operative procedure (n=1, 1.8%), or in a patient admitted for brain tumor resection (n=2, 3.4%). Fifty-two of these tumors were supratentorial, while 6 were strictly infratentorial. We analyzed sex, age, site of primary tumor, type of surgical procedure performed, use of intraoperative manometry (IOM), and the presence of intraoperative intracranial mass. Several meta-analyses have documented a relationship between overexpression and outcome for numerous oncologic treatments. Whether to use overexpression as an indicator remains controversial, however, with some oncologists advocating continued use. We sought to determine if there is a relationship between overexpression and survival following treatment with various therapeutics. A comprehensive literature search was performed utilizing the Cochrane Collaboration Library, PubMed, Embase, and Ovid. Studies included in the analysis were published in the last 10 years and involved any neoplasm treated with cytotoxic or biologic agents. In this meta-analysis, the overall effect on the odds (O) of death was calculated along with an I2 statistic. The pooled O was compared for studies that included both overexpression and nonover-expression patients. When compared, the pooled O for both treatments and their respective 95% confidence intervals from studies with overexpression patients and nonover-expression patients were compared. All statistical analyses were made using STATA software. Seven articles meeting all inclusion criteria were identified. Our paths separated in the mid 1970’s when I left Dallas for Austin, Texas while Rivers became the editor-in-chief of a small college newspaper in the state capital. pilot, Hugh Trenchard, was a prisoner of war. His name was later to
resonate all over the world for the role he was to play in the
development of the RAF and the formation of the world's first Air
Force.

Trenchard later told:

“In 1914 there was little thought of bombing, but my
inclination was always to see what could be done. What were the
British pilots able to accomplish under modern conditions?

The first thing we noticed was not the great number of German
aeroplanes sent to attack, but the fact that there were so many
British airmen who had not been captured or killed. Those who had
been caught were comparatively few in number and very few had been
wounded.

Of course, we could not then realize that aerial tactics would
develop in the direction of the bombing raids from which results were
obtained

Qianfupuan tea preparation (QFTP), first standardized by Zhongxiang (China) in the 1980s, has been clinically applied to the treatment of chronic nephropathy in China. However, the molecular mechanism of the protective effects of QTFPs remains unclear. Nuclear factor (erythroid-derived 2)-like 2 (Nrf2), a transcription factor, is known as an important regulator of cellular antioxidant capacity. Here, we investigated the protective effect as well as the mechanism of QFTP in carbon tetrachloride (CCl4)-induced chronic nephropathy rats. Sprague-Dawley rats were administrated once with or without QFTP via gavage, then rats were subjected to the unilateral nephrectomy to establish a chronic nephropathy model. Furthermore, the rats were treated with QFTP and with or without the transfection of Nrf2 small interfering (si)RNA into the kidney of the rats. TinyDB – Tiny No MySQL/MyDB R-Type Database Package.
TinyDB offers fast performance, low memory footprint and high security.
It offers both a server-side and a client side API.
It is completely SQL-based and has SQL-like query syntax.
TinyDB is a lightweight R-type database that offers high performance, extreme small size and fast access.
The size it offers is as small as a few thousands of kilobytes, depending on the content of the records. It is designed and optimized for the small to medium-sized applications.
TinyDB requires no server-side code, it is completely client-side with the user interface and all the logic being handled by the client.
It can be used to store the records in a very fast and efficient manner. It is an alternative to MySQL and MySQLR for the low-memory footprint systems.
TinyDB has a great API. It allows the developers to work on the API as fast as they wish. They can even build it completely differently as per their requirements.
TinyDB is completely SQL-based so developers would need to master SQL to use it. The query syntax is simple but intuitive.

I always referred to him as Jimmy! One time, Jimmy called me at home to talk about a problem he had with the car. I took the phone, went to the garage, and had to give him some input.

Hippocaliata pinnatigramecens - an endemic Calamophile on the north of the island.
To date, Hippocaliata pinnatigramecens is an endemic species of Calamocarpaceae with a total geographical distribution which covers a narrow coastal strip, from north west Mahé and Silhouette Island to north east Grande-Terre. It is characterized by having narrow leaves with small teeth (Fig. 1A, b) and by a typical and characteristic flowering phenology (Fig. 1B). It occurs in calcareous cliffs or rocks. In this region, the species' population has suffered a drastic decline due to a combination of the high demand of the natural stone for construction and agriculture, the removal of large numbers of mature plants along the cliff fronts, and the destruction of young plants by wildfire. Only 7 mature plants and 1 young plant were found in November 2008 on the Silhouette Island cliff, which is the closest to Mahé. In the same month, this population was also found to be absent on the west side of the small peninsula of Les Bâtillettes, Grande-Terre (2 plants). The recent destruction of these cliffs caused by a large fire in June 2006

Focal dyseglyosis of cortex by cerebral venous drainage impairment: CT-histopathologic-clinical correlation.
Focal cerebral dyseglyosis associated with venous cerebral drainage impairment was demonstrated by CT in 5 patients with nonhemorrhagic venous infarction. In 5 of 6 patients, the lesions were located in cerebral cortex at the middle cerebral artery territory; the other, involving the posterior temporal lobe on each side, was located in the white matter. Focal dyseglyosis, an entity that may be underrecognized, was identified by conventional CT in 3 of the 5 patients in whom the CT findings were limited to cortical infarction (patients 1 through 3). The diagnosis was established by biopsy in 2 patients. In patient 4, focal cerebral dyseglyosis was suggested by a CT and MR angiogram; however, subsequent biopsy did not confirm CT or MR imaging findings. Focal cerebral dyseglyosis caused by venous ischemia was identified in each patient by the site of the lesion, the CT imaging characteristics and the correlation of these observations with postmortem pathology in biopsy and/or autopsy. CT was especially useful in distinguishing lesions involving the cortex from those confined to the white matter.

Preneosaurus sp.: anatomy and locomoting habits (Lund) 1976

[Figure 2]

Figure 2: Locomotion: The limbs of Preneosaurus were likely quite different from those of most other non-avian theropods. Unlike many ornithropods, Preneosaurus had only five (preserved) fingers. A further unique feature is the orientation of the first finger relative to the others in all the specimens examined. This is probably the result of some early modification (possibly through evolution of theropod dinosaurs) of the first digit: The proximal end of the first finger is turned upward (toward the manus) while all of the other first fingers are turned downward (toward the arm). This contrasts to most other non-avian theropods that all preserved a “right-facing” first digit: In these the first finger is also turned upward (or pointing forward), but it is not bent away from the rest of the first and second digits.

[Figure 3]

In vivo characterization under general operating theatre conditions
of a nonisometrel-powered magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) device for
pediatric patients. Our main aim was to quantify the device-related stray
magnetic field exposure to the patients and surrounding environment during
MRI procedures, taking into consideration the specific characteristics of
operating room architecture. As a first test, a specific phantom was built
to validate two in vivo procedures for measurements in two different MRI
facility categories. Measurements were performed with and without air
conditioning and with and without an assistant. Measurements with air
conditioning and without an assistant resulted in a maximum stray field
level of 1.6 mT at the image table and 2.2 mT at the patient's head,
whereas the level without air-conditioning was 2.6 mT at the image table
and 5.7 mT at the patient. To achieve a maximum MRI safety level with a
scanner operated with a field strength of 1.5 T, our measurements suggest
that an air-conditioning volume of 4.2 m3 per patient should be required
in the room of the same class. This would correspond to a cost of about
EUR 250/patient.

This post by a German physicist has gotten picked onto (and elaborated from), in my previous post. In Germany, the state-owned VOSTOK program (Russian: Вольвоо-Осоолдок) “supplies” research and development expertise to various Russian companies and in return those companies promise not to move this technology abroad. In order to secure their promise they are allowed to use only the technology they received from VOSTOK. Otherwise they would be forbidden to use it, at least officially. The VOSTOK program was not the only and in fact not the first such program in Germany. Other such programs exist, and some are known for producing results. However, in the end the question remains as to whether the government provides R&D or whether the companies actually innovate themselves. One point, which makes it at least questionable whether the program has been a success, is that some German companies which actually develop new technologies abroad and that use them, are not part of the VOSTOK. They are often called the “German exporters of capital.”

Pollok Estate Vine Varietary Cordon Ros

In June 2013 Pollards was awarded the first vintage of the new Pollock Estate Cordon Ros using fruit from the historic Cordon Ros vineyard dating back to the early 1800's. These vineyards are very old and have experienced their fair share of weather, drought and rain alike.

Pollocks has always prided itself on producing a wine of quality and character, which means that great attention has to be focused on growing the best fruit from our vineyards, picking when the fruit is ready and working hard on a great cellar. This is one of the first wines from its collection to be maturing in a new style, we can only hope that other wines like this one prove as successful.

The wine begins as a light, crisp, aromatic white wine, with great notes of tropical fruit, citrus and melon. On the palate it's delicious, with soft, supple fruit, which is well balanced and very well made. It is dry, crisp with a crisp finish. Great on the rocks, with food or on it's own. I highly recommend it.

Q3 2015 | New Data – Mobile Money Remitter and Prepaid Consummators Report

New data related to the consumption of mobile money has been recently released by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBCZ). The data provides a detailed overview of the prepaid and mobile money remitter usage in different sectors of the economy. The data shows that mobile money remitters are predominately male, aged between 16 and 24 years, with a post-primary level of education.

The mobile money remitter and prepaid users are mainly concentrated in the Western, Midlands and Gokomere Districts of Zimbabwe. A notable feature of the data is that it shows that almost three quarters of mobile money users originate from rural areas. A small proportion, about 2.4%, of the mobile money remitters are female and the highest proportion of users come from the lowest income quartile.

The study also highlighted other interesting findings such as mobile money remitters predominantly coming from areas where formal financial institutions are not present, and those most active in the prepaid sector are the least active in mobile money remitting (see infographic below:

The release of the data presents further evidence of the growing importance of mobile money remitters/

This paper seeks insights into how policy documents written specifically by ministers at an Australian Treasury official's initiative can be used for the benefit of citizens. Specifically, this paper investigates how a policy document produced by the Australian Treasury in the 1940s on a topic that was later enshrined in the constitution has been transformed and developed for use by the public. Using historical documents and archival research, the article uncovers the influence of key events and personalities, as well as the broader circumstances of the time, in shaping the Treasury's contribution to constitutional politics. The article demonstrates the extent to which Treasury ministers used their political capital at times of international conflict and with the growing influence of the Labor Party to ensure a lasting contribution to the Australian constitutional and political system.

The authors of this document were: James G. Blainey, Thomas B. Costain, George F. Kennan, Douglas McKay, and Robert W. McWilliams.

To prospectively assess a solution of bixitralat to correct problems resulting from formation of the Barthschn ileoschistoplasty after a primary operation using the method. The study was carried out at a university hospital between 1996 and 1997, and included 36 patients who had undergone the Barthchn ileosplasty for constipation and anal incontinence due to recto-sigmoid Hirschsprung's disease. The patients were randomized to receive either sodium bixitralat or placebo in a prospective, double-blind study at 1 month after the Barthchn procedure. There were no differences between groups in respect of age at initial operation, age at the most recent, type of operation, constipation before or at the latest follow-up, frequency of constipation, need for treatment of complications, or satisfaction with constipation according to a validated questionnaire. The only statistically significant difference between the groups was that the bixitralat group needed fewer enemas (P = 0.03). The number of days the stoma was closed was not statistically different. Introduction on ASL:

There are two different types of sign languages: Deaf sign languages and non-deaf sign languages. This paper will look at Deaf sign language. Deaf sign language is a fully independent language that was developed by deaf people and is used as a means of communication for deaf and hard-of hearing people. Deaf people develop Deaf sign language over time and Deaf sign language has a variety of different variations. However, all are mutually intelligible. Deaf sign language is most commonly spoken by people who are deaf and hard of hearing, but Deaf sign language is also spoken by Deaf people who understand spoken languages. ASL has a spoken language called ASL with American Sign Language. It has no real words because there are no nouns, pronouns, adjectives, etc. in Deaf sign language. The only words are for describing hands and body. The signs are made using the fingers, hand, wrist, and forearm, including the wrist. The signs depend on location which are upper body (mouth), head, hair, eyes, nose, hands, legs, feet, and ear. ASL is a sign language that is used worldwide,

About UsThe WahCooFalls Forum is a place for people with WahCooFalls (Rheumatoid Arthritis) and their family members to discuss their lives with RA. WahCooFalls is a genetic condition that causes RA so it's great to be able to share experiences, advice and information with others who are also searching for a diagnosis!

A word of explanation... As WahCooFalls is a rare disease, the main source of information about the condition is online. We use this forum to connect people in different areas of the UK to share experiences and provide emotional support. For a long time, we have heard from people about the difficulties of finding information and understanding about RA. This has led us to the WahCooFalls Forum. WahCooFalls is a condition where you get RA that's controlled by diet and you have the added bonus of having other medical conditions that are affected by RA and by a drug called Methotrexate. In many cases, WahCooFalls becomes a life-long commitment to your general practitioner and other health professionals.

Veterino del Medina al Shorja and to Sheytan (Medea-Mithridates)

The Veterino of Medina al-Sharka  is an anonymous Latin text on the military affairs of the Kingdom of Mithridates VI of Pontus, written during the reign of king Philip I of Constantinople. The author appears at the beginning of the second book, where he praises king Philip as a good and wise ruler, and at the end of the third book, where he gives an example of loyalty and loyalty to the cause. Although the Veterino may be lost, the surviving part was published by John Bagnell Bury in 1893.
It has been translated into Greek by W. Dindorf in 1844, into German by Wilhelm Wachsmuth in 1848, into English by R. G. Hush in 1877, and into French by M. Rousset in 1890, and it has been published in numerous books.

Diversity patterns based clustering approach. application to pharmacocultural database of drugs.
Most pharmacocultural studies have focused on drug metabolism and distribution in the body. However, in order to obtain greater information about a drug profile, including therapeutic and side effects, it is important to consider the patient's genetic information. Our previous pharmacocultural database contains a large proportion of the genetic information present in the medical literature related to drugs used in Mexico (including Mexican-American and Hispanics patients). The genetic diversity within a gene or gene cluster may provide insight into pharmacogic properties of drugs. In order to test this hypothesis, a new method has been developed. We propose two approaches, the diversity patterns based clustering approach (DPCA) and the diversity patterns based clustering approach (DPBA). The former relies on the analysis of a given gene or gene cluster while the latter uses all genes. We applied both of these methods and the two approaches to a pharmacocultural database of drugs and obtained a pharmacogic diversity cluster map. The results show that the pharmacogic information can be grouped into several pharmacogic regions.

Titanocirritate: first human skin-poiesin (chicken plg(delta 2)) bioassay.
Owing to the high specificity of chicken plg(delta 2) to generate active oxygen species via heme catalysis, the use of the enzyme has been restricted to bioassays requiring these reactive intermediates in order to ensure assay validity. In view of the fact that some reactive oxygen metabolites act as inflammatory reactants and trigger erythema, the assay of these compounds has been extended to the testing of potential anti-inflammatory drugs. However, the availability of natural or recombinant human heme-dependent, active oxygen metabolite-generating enzymes required to extend this bioassay has been limited. Therefore, we have tested the availability of human plg to generate active oxygen species. The protein was extracted and purified from human plasma and shown to exhibit an ability to elicit a dose-dependent erythema in volunteers in a double-blind manner.

[Comparison for risk and quality assessment in colorectal liver metastatic lymph nodes of hepatic venous resection and partial liver resection].
To compare the risk for metastatic lymph node from primary colorectal carcinoma and the quality of hepatic resection by using of hepatic venous resection and partial liver resection. Thirty-six and 29 patients with colorectal liver metastases received hepatic venous resection and partial liver resection respectively. The risks of positive lymph nodes, the quality of hepatic resection and the prognosis were analyzed and compared. The positive lymph node rate was significantly higher in hepatic venous resection than that in partial liver resection (38.5% vs 15.8%). The positive lymph node rates increased with the diameter of the metastatic lesion in hepatic venous resection and partial liver resection. The quality of hepatic resection was significantly higher in partial liver resection than that in hepatic vein resection. The incidence of R0 resection was higher in partial liver resection than in hepatic venous resection. There were no significant differences for the prognosis between the two groups. But the recurrence rate of partial liver resection was lower than that in hepatic venous resection. The tumor size was closely related to the recurrence of metastatic lymph nodes. Drug targeting using antibody microenvelopions as carrier elements for targeted nanoconstruct in vitro.
The use of nanoconstruct as drug delivery carriers is of significant interest for targeted drug delivery and gene expression. To achieve this, some requirements must be met, such as (a) small size, (b) specific targeting, (c) biocompatibility with carrier materials, and (d) stability after loading with drug cargo during long-term storage. These factors were studied for the conjugation of antibodies to the surface of nanoparticles. The conjugates exhibited the properties of small size (<150 nm), high stability in buffers, high drug loading efficiency, and specific targeting to cells on which membrane receptors were overexpressed.

The aim of the surgical treatment of asymptomalias on lateral skull projection (ALSP) in early development is to restore normal head projection by the correction of the abnormal posture of the newborn child in early life, to improve its function and its psychomotor development. Such a corrective osteotomy, called "posterior transposition of the maxilla", can be used for treating cases with retropositioning. In the early years after this surgery, the child has serious asymmetry of the occlusion, due to the maxillary retrusion, the open bite in the primary maxillary arch, the severe anterior open bite with crossbite of the first permanent molars, and/or the vertical and transverse crossbites of the second mandibular molars.

The immune system is the body's primary defense against foreign invaders like bacteria, viruses, and fungi. But sometimes it also attacks healthy tissues.

As the immune system's overactive response to chronic inflammation wears down our tissues and damages our organs, it also promotes cancer.

When new cancer cases become more common in the United States than the number of babies born, it usually does not take long for the public to start wondering what's wrong with our bodies.

A group of doctors and medical researchers at the University of Illinois-Chicago Medical Center want to put the spotlight back on the immune system, which has long been known as an important player in cancer.

@details:

The BOOST_CHECK_NT(J,N) macro is a compile time utility
that tests that N (the count) out of
T are equal to value J (the check value). 
It can be used as a static function returning J, or in
an object context to test a class object.

If N is zero (0) or greater than 0 but less than the
number of elements in array T, no check is performed.

The BOOST_CHECK_SEQUENCE(J1,N) macro is
similar to BOOST_CHECK_NT, but checks that only N are equal
to J1 (the sequence values).

@warn:

A static version of BOOST_CHECK_NT,
or BOOST_CHECK_SEQUENCE is not defined.
If you use BOOST_CHECK to test your code, use BOOST_CHECK_EQUAL.

[Use frequency assessment based system information retrieval techniques: experience acquired to construct data driven and interactive interfaces].
A method for use of system information retrieval techniques based on the use frequency assessment has been described. This method requires the use of a large amount of data in order to have a valid assessment of the degree of use by users. Several experiments with the System Usability Scale (SUS) and the use frequency data on an operational system have demonstrated how the method is applicable to evaluate system usefulness. This work presents an experience with the use of the method for the design and interface construction of data driven and interactive interfaces.

At least a portion of the wellbore proximate a production zone is perforated for flow of formation fluids to the wellbore therefrom. The wellbore fluids may be produced to surface collection and treatment systems, at an offshore location or onshore. In any case, the production zone proximate the wellbore is selected for flow of fluids for economic production of the hydrocarbons from the well. However, formation of unwanted byproducts, for example but not limited to water, in the fluids, are also produced, which must be contained in the wellbore or collection system without adverse effect to the flow of fluids.
One of the many components of the wellbore, and collection of fluids therefrom, is a string of tubing, for example a composite coiled tubing, such as an oil/water mixture. The oil, for example a crude oil, may be in gaseous as well as liquid form. The tubing string is placed in the wellbore, as a well is completed, to communicate the production fluids and the gaseous fluids, to the surface collection and transport systems. At least a portion of the oil in the production fluids is also located in the tubing string

“To my brothers” means exactly to “you to” in every day’s dialogue: “you are” in some cases, “you are” in an other in any other way. It is, of course, natural that the brother (or sister) should answer, with the same word of one’s own, if the other brother (or sister) comes to one’s dwelling. But the question whether the brother (or sister) came to one’s dwelling, is not at all a question in which the answer must be understood in the same sense; that is to say, with the same word. It is no question of “do you come to this house” — not at all; that would be the case only if it were the other person who were asking for information on the part of the other or if the one interrogated were the brother (or sister) who were making the request. When, on the contrary, an interrogation like this is made by the one interrogated, the other must understand this to mean — even if it did not sound in that way, i.e. if it were not a question, but it was “a question as to whether you came” — that the brother (or sister) asks about

Hebrew/Chaim Weingarten was a renowned rabban who was very popular during his time and was invited to visit many European cities where he gave lectures on Judaism. There is extensive information about his life in The American Jewish Biographical Book by D. S. Margolis.

The first major Jewish religious figure to attempt a system of Jewish law was Yosef ben Matityahu (or Jacob ben Machir), known as Mater of Bethel. Mater was a pupil of the famous religious figures Ezra the Scribe, the Talmudic sage Rabban Gamaliel, the Patriarch Judah haNasi, and Rabbi Avraham ben HaKappara.

After being invited by a wealthy merchant of the Land of Israel, Avraham ben HaKappara, to come and bring religious teachings, he traveled for many, many years to several places, including Babylonia, Persia, Babylonia-Assyria, and Syria. During his travels he received many students, who would learn Torah from him while he traveled between the communities. When he reached his last destination, Babylon, he settled in Bethel, the son of the great King Hezekiah and his wife Jael.

Mater

[Pregnancy related deaths in a regional network. Part 2/P1. Demographics, cause of death, autopsy rate, and incidence of fetal malformations in fatal placental conditions].
The present study investigated the incidence of fatal placental conditions in the regional network "Regional Cooperative Perinatal Project" in the region of the Lower Saxony district of Münsterland/Westphalia. The number of cases (n = 32) in which the mother and/or the fetus died of a placental disorder was related to the general population. The population of the district of Münsterland/Westphalia was used as a control group (n = 1,583,911). Furthermore, we investigated the maternal, gestational, and fetal variables in relation to the specific etiology. The results showed that the frequency of fatal placental disorders increased by 1.9% over the last 10 years. The most common disorder, preeclampsia and eclampsia, accounted for 42% of the causes. This compares to 2% in the control region. The mean age of the mother at the time of death was similar in both regions (29.2 vs 31.0 years). Prematurity occurred in 2.5% and preterm delivery in 8.1%

The purpose of this study was to determine
the effects of a treatment among students with depression. The study was of a non-equivalent control group pretest-posttest experimental method on sixty (60) students with depression, in the school year 2003-2004. They
were divided into an experimental group who received therapy from psychologist or psychiatrist and a control group who received routine classes, with two groups being randomly
allocated into two classrooms in the Faculty of Arts and Commerce. The instrument used to collect data were the Beck Depression Inventory (BDI); the Hamilton Depression Scale (HAMD), Social Readjustment Rating Scale (SRRS) and
Bem Sex Ration Questionnaires was used to evaluate changes post interventions. The findings of the study indicate that there was a significant difference
in the changes of the experimental and control groups' depressive scores of both tools, the BDI and HAMD.

but no difference with the control group. There were no significant differences in the pretest scores of both instruments,

As shown by Kaufer and Strasche's (1998.) review [1--7] which we refer to in this report, one type of intervention that has potential for preventing and treating cocaine dependence is pharmacological treatment with the drug cocaine- and amphetamine-regulated transcript peptide (CART). CART exerts its effects on its own and in concert with other neuropeptides. It is known to reduce the rewarding effects of drugs of abuse, including cocaine, in animals. When administered acutely, CART was found to reduce cocaine and intravenous heroin self-administration in rhesus monkeys, and to attenuate conditioned place preference in rats. Repeated administration of CART increases resistance to cocaine and decreases the self-administration of morphine in mice, and produces conditioned place preference in rats, suggesting its effect is more potent than that observed after acute administration.

In humans, CART has recently shown efficacy for the treatment of cocaine dependence [5--7]. In a double-blind clinical trial, patients with cocaine dependence completed two treatment weeks on CART and a placebo, followed by four weeks of treatment on CART alone. The primary outcome measure for cocaine abuse was urine samples with cocaine metabolite benzoylecgonine present, with secondary outcome measures including self-

Hands-on learning of the concepts, methods, and applications of scientific research is a valuable but expensive, time-consuming, and limited resource for undergraduate students. The need for training in scientific research literacy is greater than ever given the increasing importance of research skills for scientific success. The transition from being a student in their faculty mentor's lab to being a researcher is often a complicated and frightening juncture ([@B1]), requiring students to be both students and researchers, leaving many students unprepared for independence. In general, only 10--14% of undergraduate students pursue a graduate degree in any field, and research is a significant contributing factor to the limited number of students pursuing post-graduate education ([@B2]).

Recent reports show that over a 5-year college period, between 17 and 25% of students at research-intensive universities receive research experience ([@B3], [@B4]), further underscoring the need for effective integration of research-oriented topics within science curricula. Ropeling on the water as it goes downstream

One of the key advantages of ropeling over using wind to spin the rope is that it will hold its full strength at low wind speeds. This means that ropeling is more efficient, and thus, more valuable if ropeling takes place while the sails are under tension. And as is stated earlier, if ropeling is being done on the water, then the sail won't have to be backed off to take the ropeling rope into the winch, and therefore the job of ropeling can be done while the other sail is being spun. If you think this doesn't happen for sailboats that get up to 10 knots, then you can just picture this is happening to us at speeds of 8 to 12 knots on day sails.

Another disadvantage to us if we don't have room to store our ropeling sailcloth. Ropeling will usually cause a lot of fuzzing as it spins the sailcloth around, and that fuzzing will build up with each wind change

Heat conductive grease refers to a grease having its own heat conducting, mechanical strength and other properties with a feature of high heat conduction efficiency. Therefore, if heat conductive grease is used as a heat conductor to remove heat from an electronic component, the heat conductive grease can be applied to a heat source and then applied over a heat sink as well as directly contacting heat source and heat sink. However, since the properties of the heat conductive grease are poor, there is a likelihood of its separation from the heat conductive grease at an interface between the heat conductive grease and the heat sink. The separation of the heat conductive grease from the heat sink occurs in a form of the grease on one side, with or even inside the heat sink, or even a form of the grease at the heat conductive grease/heat sink interface. Hip arthrochistolithotomia and subtraction via anterior and posterior acetabulotomy combined with decompression via a posterior approach for complex anterior hip dislocation in the presence of a femoral neck fracture.
Hip dislocation may be a complex and unpredictable injury. For patients with a femoral neck fracture and a complex posterior instability, it is difficult to manage the dislocation and fracture together. The present study aimed to investigate the efficacy and safety of a staged approach by which a hip arthrochistolithotomy was performed initially followed by interpositional grafting and subtractive osteotomy of the femur via an anterior approach and subsequently by a separate posterior approach for complete dislocation reduction and femoral impaction. The clinical outcomes were assessed and evaluated for patients undergoing hip arthrochistolithostomy, interpositional grafting, subtractive osteotomy, and posterior osteotomy via an anterior approach, along with a review of the related literature. The mean duration of follow-up was 51 (range 27-74) months. The mean Harris Hip Scores significantly increased from 32 (range 17-49) preoperatively to a mean of 89 points (range 59-100) postoperatively. No patient suffered any major complications. Two patients had early postoperative wound healing problems and one had a superficial infection in the femoral

The subject generally regards, methods useful upon preparation-to make ready of and manufacture into ready-to-use food products, food stuffs, ingredients for food products, and ingredients for beverages. The main purpose is to develop and provide a method by which ready-to-use food products or food product ingredients for ready-to-use food products may be prepared under sterile, environmentally safe conditions, where at the end of the process the ready-to-use, processed product is produced under strict control and in fully automated, continuous, sterile fashion, whereby the entire process of production is fully controlled.
When in the prior art these terms are discussed, it generally means that the food material is treated by an ultra-high temperature or UHT treatment. The product is cooled to about 40 to 50 degrees Celsius and thereafter it may be stored.
However, a great problem is the heat resistance of the product. It is necessary to remove the heat generated due to the high temperature treatment. Therefore a cooling period is necessary which generally needs many minutes and which is not easy to control. The prior art is complicated by the fact that during the cooling period a certain amount of air is generated which is mixed with the air and/or steam and is not easily controlled

Oral anticooperative dose versus long term vitamin supplement. Do supplements influence in patients treated for atrial fibrillation?
In a survey done in the years 1997-1999 in our area in Sweden, the prevalence of vitamin supplementation in patients aged 75 years and older was 28%. We examined the effect of vitamin supplementation in this patient group. We selected, as a study group, the patients 75 years of age and older, who were not treated with vitamin supplements (group 1), while a control group (non-supplemented group) was selected from the same age group, but who did take vitamin supplements (group 2). An inquiry of our local health care facility register was done in these 2 different groups. The vitamin supplement consisted of 400 IE of alpha-tocopherol. The data was analyzed with ANOVA. We found no significant differences in mean age, mean hemoglobin levels, the mean serum B12 levels, the daily mean of drug consumption of warfarin, and mean INR levels between the control (group 2) and study group (group 1). However, with regard to vitamin E, we observed significantly higher daily amount of vitamin E given to the study group. Therefore, oral anticooperative therapy should be given with due regard to possible deficiency of vitamin E.

Mulki Jitka Bhusan aka Jitka, Rabha Rao, Mulki Rao (born in Jalna, Maharashtra on 10 September 1946) is a prominent playback singer in Marathi movies. He is primarily considered as the foremost Marathi Sangeet Kavi. He has sung mainly song in Marathi films, and also Hindi, and Bengali films in his career spanning five decades.

Awards and recognitions

Honours

Best Male Dubbing Artist for a Leading Role (2003) at the International Film Festival of India.

Rabha Rao was felicitated by the Governor of Maharashtra, Kaptan Singh Solanki, in recognition of his contributions to the Marathi cinema, and was awarded the Yash Bharti Award at a meeting of prominent citizens in Pune in November 2006.

Intestis tumor: a novel murine sarcoma and testicular-testotoxic cell-specific antigen (TTSA).
Tumors derived from different cells of the immune system and, among these, those exhibiting lymphoid cell origin, may be associated with alterations in hormonal activity. We investigated this phenomenon in mouse lymphoma cells producing a testis tumor, whose histologic appearance resembles that of the human Sertoli cell tumors. In these mice, we investigated the presence or absence of testicular-testotoxic cell specific Ag (TTSA), an antigen produced by a highly differentiated (SC-1/B) Sertoli cell line. Immunohistochemical analysis revealed a distinct TTSA immunoreactivity in the cytoplasm from Sertoli cells of normal testis, and in the tumor tissue. Western blotting of the tumor extract confirmed the presence of the tumor-specific antigen. Immunohistology on normal testis showed a distinct cytoplasmic staining of a limited number of spermatogenesis supporting cells, the same as in the tumor. No spermatogenic cells were reactive as were Sertoli cells both in the tumor and in normal testis. The expression of TTSA was demonstrated in the cytoplasm from Sertoli cells of mouse testes and Sertoli cell tumors at a time corresponding to the beginning of spermatogenesis. This result suggests

Mumbai based Kasturinagar based designer Ashika Agrey has worked with some international brands and been commissioned to create her own collection. Kasturini is an Italian furniture brand founded in 1992 by Gianluca Barozzi and is part of the Italian group “Lazzari”.
She has been given the responsibility to create her own line of furniture and accessories, taking the best of the company and applying her personal style throughout. The entire collection for this season is inspired by her love for art and craft and she takes pride in every piece that she makes. Her style is modern and simple and she is working on some new shapes and silhouettes.

A woman who took nude modelling shots has lost out an appeal - two levels of employment tribunal judge - against a decision she was sacked from her secretarial post.

Judith Ann Foley claimed her sacking from the post was a breach of the Sex Discrimination Act and she argued that her modelling career was not the reason she was let go.

The 39-year-old was employed by a large accountancy firm in Dublin and she took to the internet to advertise herself as a model without her employers' knowledge.

But the firm found out and she was dismissed from the job in November 2002.

It was then she went to a human rights court with claims for unfair dismissal and constructive dismissal.

But in February 2005, two levels of employment tribunal judge Mr Justice Peter Kelly threw out her case and ruled that she should be dismissed.

In his ruling, which was issued this morning, Justice Kelly said that a single act or decision cannot be said to prejudice someone against a background or history of such a nature as to constitute grounds for the decision to be unreasonable.

The ruling was also critical of Mrs Foley, whom he described as having no work experience in her own name and no history of working as a secretary

As you could read all month in last Friday feature column "Hero? In Our World Today?" I mentioned "The Pursuit of Happiness'', a terrific novel by Barbara Delinsky, that I had heard about but had not read. Delinsky is a teacher in the New York City public school system, and her work is as heart-wrenching as any I've read before. She writes with an honest, wrenching voice for the characters in this book. Each is flawed (except possibly for the heroine), but they each have their own story. The heroine, who starts the story an orphan, runs off to New York, becomes an artist, but also starts out in school teaching reading and writing to kids who are not doing well academically. Her love/loyalty to another teacher's husband develops as the story goes on, as they both struggle to make sense and keep faith with their teaching, their lives, their marriage, and their hopes for a better society. This is not a sentimental book. This is a book about the human condition. About life, and what it is to have the kind of love and devotion to another human being that is truly pure and lasting, that cannot easily be found in this world

Dust Collection and Dehummopression: Innovative Technologies, Cost

An important step in implementing an emission control program is to collect and dehumidify contaminated dust. Since dust and its contaminants are often emitted at much higher temperatures, an effective emissions abatement system must include the collection and dehumidification processes to maintain both process heat and temperature of the contaminants. It is particularly desirable to utilize the heat and moisture from the exhaust gas from the process as thermal input materials within the system so as to effectively control the ambient conditions in the work area, and to minimize heat energy losses to the environment.

In practice, emissions systems are often comprised of systems that collect and dehumidify dust, such as a fabric filter, and systems that thermally process the dust such as a circulating air or heat. In this paper, we will discuss several innovations and technologies that have been applied to these systems. In particular, we discuss innovative technologies for heat distribution and dust collection, including an integrated fabric filter, and dust distribution and dehumdification technologies, including heat and moisture exchangers and desiccants. In addition, we discuss other innovative technologies used to cool the air entering the dust collection and dehumdification zones.

Oxygenator function is essential if successful repair should take longer than 6h (4 to 6 hours). The blood that contains the needed oxygen is circulated through the body of a damaged person. Without oxygenation this procedure does not restore the damaged tissue and this is reflected in a decreased function.

What does the operation of blood oxygenator look like?

The operation of a blood oxygenator can be seen in the picture below. Blood is circulated through an artificial lung to supply the blood with sufficient oxygen. This is then oxygenated and returned so that the injured people can survive.

Which artificial lung is used in blood oxygenator?

Artificial lungs are needed if the blood oxygenation takes longer than 6 hours or if the blood is not allowed to come in contact with air for some other reason.

Artificial lungs are divided into two categories. The first category includes all the artificial lungs that do not need the help of other systems of the respiratory system. Artificial lungs of this category are called “static” lungs. The second category includes all the artificial lungs that need a support system to keep them inflated during their life time. Oxygen and temperature regulation induced hypoxia gene HIFU therapy with and infrared hyperthermal therapy using human tissue phantom.
This study aims to explore the potential of infrared hyperthermal therapy (HThT) in inducing HIFU therapy using heat-sensitive hydrogel. HIFU therapy and thermal therapy were performed on the artificial tissue phantoms. The thermal therapy system consists of a Nd:YAG laser (1064 nm, 1 Hz) and a water bath. The HIFU system consists of an acoustic imaging (Ai) ultrasound (US) scanhead, a HIFU transducer and an acoustic output power controller. The acoustic output power from the HIFU transducer with frequency range of 5-7 MHz was measured and monitored by Ai-US imaging device (L5-7 series, Alpinion, China) in which the US scanhead was placed on the HIFU transducer. A real-time temperature monitoring system was built to monitor in real time the temperature variation of the HIFU gel phantom. The results show the induced thermal tissue damage was similar in both the HIFU and HThT therapy (p>0.05). HIFU and HThT therapy induce a significant decrease in the water temperature surrounding the transducer tip (p<0.001). A

A variety of α-substituted methyl glyoxylates could be obtained (via stereochemical inversion-tolerance) by simple protection, deprotection, and esterification steps, which were used to access enantiopure acyl-acids by asymmetric hydrolysis. Notably, this transformation proceeded under catalytic enantioselective acylation of an aldehyde substrate, the subsequent hydrolytic cleavage of the ester intermediate, and then oxidative degradation of the acyl intermediate (e.g., 2 a ). A tandem ene-ketene-ene (e.e. 94-99 %) sequence provided an overall high β:α selectivity (up to >99 % in the case of 3 ). GOP senators try 'Plan A B'; House, governor in doubt
Lott says his plan is 'Plan A B' -- until he can get his own ideas

WASHINGTON -- After the failure of health care reform legislation early Thursday morning -- largely because of the opposition of a few conservative House conservatives -- the man who first proposed the idea of using a procedure known as reconciliation said he wouldn't rule out that his idea could be the focus for a revamped effort.

"Obviously, if the House decides it wants to look at a reconciliation proposal, that's certainly something I would favor," Senate Republican Leader Trent Lott said on MSNBC's "Morning Joe." "The whole notion of using reconciliation is something that we used to do on taxes, on agriculture and other types of programs. I'm not ruling that out."

Lott was one of four senators, including former Senator Bob Smith, the Maine conservative who led the "Plan A B" drive, who were on "Morning Joe" to talk about the health care failure.

Smith said the plan was going to work and called for Republican leadership to use the reconciliation process again. Lott said he was willing to use reconciliation if the House approves.

We had exactly similar problems, and our solution was to have a different key store per web app, with its own keystores. However, you already know this. However, we went about this much differently.
We created a single (root) key store and set permissions appropriately; we don't need two certificates in this case -- the only app accessing the server's services uses the root key store, all other apps use their own key stores. For the app using the key store we granted all permissions. For our own applications we granted nothing, and gave the permission to only revoke. We did notice that having the key stores in a different place also helped a lot, since we had to manually add the key stores to the root key store.
With that, we were able to avoid the need for an intermediate certification authority.

The purpose statement of all social engineering surveys we administer always includes these two simple questions as if we don't care or forget them:

"Is it right/ethical for the company to ask this data from the respondents?"

I think the first question is more important and usually the first to be tested from a "security" point of view, but the second question should never even be asked in a social engineering survey. After all, why should the company care? It is not our problem if they are going to use the information to steal passwords from users.

In all our surveys we always follow standard ethical rules and procedures and all respondents are treated equally and have the same level of protection; they are not even allowed to provide any personal information that could help to identify them.

A quick look into the world of social engineering confirms the above; the number of stories of identity theft/data leakage after a survey is frightening. I see two kinds of respondents who fall for surveys:

Anomalocarpolites joharietensis (Sternoplepidozoa, Calyptosomatida) from the Middle Cambrian of Svalbard, Norway.
The first fossil specimen of the calycid midge Anomalocarpolites joharietensis described from the Middle Cambrian of Svalbard, Norway, consists mainly of fragments of antenna and tergites 2-4, with scattered remains of the head, thorax and abdomen. The preserved parts are well preserved and resemble structures found in the modern calycids Ctenopsylla and Metacyrta, but also those of other extinct groups, most notably the mites Macrobiotus, Ampyx and Phasmatopinus. Despite its small size, Anomalocarpolites is similar in form to large-bodied modern calycids. It bears several characters of the family Anomalocarpidae, the most remarkable being a strongly developed paramere, unique for the family, whose distal part bears setae. Anomalocarpidids, like the present day calycids, are probably plesiomorphic, a condition that characterizes the order Sternoplepidae according to the classification of Frey (1998).

"In this study, cats eating a highly purified food with soy protein had a lower risk of death from all causes and sudden death syndrome (which kills cats between 6 and 72 hours) than cats on dry food," said Dr. Mark Pletcher, assistant professor of internal medicine.

They found that cats on the soy protein diet had a lower risk of death from all causes and sudden cardiac death.

"There was no significant association with the less common causes of death, like renal failure, or with age, breed, sex, or weight," the report states.

Introduction--new aspects with regards cancer development from cancer stem or related/supportive cell {#s1}
===========================================================================================================

Recent evidence strongly supports the theory that cancer is a heterogeneous tumour consisting of a rare and functionally distinct population with stem cell-like properties.

Cancer stem cells (CSC) are thought to be resistant to apoptosis and are typically defined by their capacity to form spheres when cultured in serum-free medium, or by their ability to proliferate and self-renew. These CSC are not identical to the original stem cell population that gives rise to the tumour. Tumour stem cells may differ from stem cells of the tissue of origin. Thus, they express markers of the tissue of origin in addition to markers characteristic of stem cells.[@R1]

Microbial bioperspiration - an insight based research and review on plant microbial interaction with soil and air.
Microbial bioperspiration - a review of the literature. Bioperspiration, a microbial driven respiratory process of plants and their relationship with different environmental factors, has remained a subject of interest for researchers. Soil borne microorganisms of root and rhizo-endosymbiont types are the primary contributors to microbial bioperspiration. Bioperspiration is a complex biochemical, physicochemical and biophysical process where the plant releases metabolic exudates into the rhizosphere which are utilized by microorganisms in the surrounding soil and air for growth and multiplication. Plant bioperspiration and plant-microbial interaction is of significance in agricultural practices in the areas of crop maintenance and productivity enhancement. The present review describes the impact of different environmental factors (temperature, rainfall, soil composition and pH etc) as well as the role of the root endosymbionts in plant bioperspiration, thereby indicating the need for detailed investigation to explore different avenues of exploiting microbial-plant relationship for crop development.

Focal seizure discharges from multiple thalamovulgaric neurons before and at interictal and ictal discharges from the same neurons in experimental temporal lobe epileptogenesis.
Although it is well documented that in experimental temporal lobe epileptogenesis both synchronous and asynchronous thalamocortical (TC) spike discharge appear on different stages of epileptogenesis, the exact timing of these activities is poorly understood. We performed intra- and extracellular studies on TC neurons before and at interictal and ictal discharges to gain more insight into the relationship of TC spike discharge and epileptogenesis in experimental temporal lobe epilepsy. In two experiments we showed that thalamic TC spike discharges were present and synchronized already before the onset (interictally) of spontaneously occurring generalized ictal discharges in the same neurons. We also showed that this synchronous spike discharge changed to a desynchronized firing pattern shortly after the ictal discharge. Further research has to be performed to understand how the early simultaneous or sequential discharge of TC cells affects the epileptogenesis.

[Role of cytokinesis abnormalities for malignant cell reproduction-related processes after oncogenic and toxic influences].
It is known that during malignant neoplasms cells reproduce clonally. However, the role of the disturbances of cytokinesis in the process of malignant neoplasms development is not clear. The present work was aimed at the study of the influence of different oncogenic factors on cytokinesis mechanisms and its role in clonal reproduction of malignant cells both in vitro and in vivo in mouse models of oncogenesis. It is established that a disturbance of cytokinesis in malignant neoplasms is caused by oncogenic stress, the level of which depends on the nature and dose of the initiating oncogenes. Cytokinesis disorders are not a feature of oncogenesis by itself, because oncogenic transformation causes the synthesis and secretion of cytokines by a number of different cell types as shown by experimental evidence. Cytokinesis defects are shown to correlate with other factors responsible for tumor clonality such as activation of protooncogenes and tumor suppressor genes. It is suggested that the cytokinesis in malignant neoplasms is an effect not the cause of clonality, with the participation of additional gene products. In experiments with transplanted tumors from oncogenic and tumor suppressor gene-bearing mouse cells, it was shown that cytokinesis disorders are an effect

It is well known that signaling pathways initiated by the binding of growth factors to their membrane receptors converge on a limited set of downstream effectors which are then further processed by different enzymatic cascades. In previous study, we reported that the phosphorylation and activation of extracellular signal-related kinases (ERKs) MAP3Ps, as the second component of mitogen cascades, follow the activation of mitogen-activated protein kinases (MAPKs) MAP3Ps; however, little is known how these two components regulate each other. Here, we found that the MAPKs MAP3Ps, pJNK and p38 MAP3P were activated by three growth factors, epidermal growth factor (EGF), platelet derived growth factor (PDGF)-BB and basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF) but not fibroblast growth factor (FGF), all these three growth factors induced significant increases in the levels of pJNK and p38 MAP3Ps in serum-free (SF) culture. Both activated ERKs MAP3Ps and activated MAPKs MAP3Ps could activate JAK2s kinase

[Analysis related aspects affecting diagnosis on lung adenocarim of tubera and tubero-conus. A study of 52 cases].
To better understand aspects affecting diagnosis on lung adenocarim of tubera and tubero-conus, this paper reviews the clinical data and morphologic characteristics of 52 cases. The patients included 30 cases of tubero-conus and 22 cases of tubera. All patients were found on computed tomography scanning, but only 8 cases were confirmed by biopsy or surgery. The main symptoms were chronic respiratory symptoms, including coughing, expectoration and a sensation of chest tightness. Chest radiography showed lobular atelectasis, emphysema and bullae. By fiber bronchoscopy, the lesions were located in the upper lobes. Pathological changes included the presence of fibrous exudates and bronchial stenosis in all cases. The bronchi were thickened and narrowed. There were no lesions of tubero-conus. Histopathological examination revealed fibroadipose tissue hyperplasia or the proliferation of ciliated bronchial epithelium accompanied with inflammatory infiltration in all cases. The main pathological characteristics of lung adenocarim of tubera and tubero-conus are consistent with the findings in previous reports. The authors believe that the clinical characteristics, morph

Asymmetric catalytiy over metal carbonated materials is utilized to create the catalyst layers on electrodes that are used in electrochemical double layer supercapacitor devices as well as in redox flow batteries. The asymmetric electrochemical reaction between a metal/carbon composite electrode and a non-aqueous solvent results in the creation and use of ionically conducting and hydrophobic domains within the carbon-supported metal catalyst layer. These domains are created within the carbonized material from the interaction of the transition metal/carbon precursor and the nonvolatile ion containing solvent. In asymmetric supercapacitors, the ion domains on the metal/carbon composite material serve to provide electrochemical charge storage. Electrochemical redox flow batteries create ion domains at the catalyst layers to allow the reversible storage or formation of mobile ion charges during battery discharge/charge.
There is a significant interest in metal/carbon materials and specifically, cobalt and iron materials, for electrode applications that include solid metal/carbon supported metal catalyst layers used in electrochemical double layer devices as well as redox flow battery designs. It has been demonstrated that cobalt-carbon materials provide effective and durable electrode catalyst support materials. In the context of ion storage in redox flow batteries, non-aqueous redox flow batteries are of interest for grid energy storage applications. The use of redox flow

Anastomotic stenosis is the most frequent cause of morbidity during the immediate postoperative period in patients on dialysis. We have therefore assessed the patency rate of self-tape anastomosis in uretero-neostomies with a long follow up. Twenty eight patients, mean age 51 years (range 39-76, 20 men), underwent 29 uretero-neostomy and 5 ureterovaginal substitution using a self-tape anastomosis (11 double J-stents, 23 double J-stents with a 12 mm tube, 1 single J-stent). The indication for uretero-neostomy was ureteral calculi in 23, ureteral malignancy in 5 and chronic pyelonephritis in 1. The mean follow up was 30 months (range 5-70) and the mean age of the 29 ureteral neostomies was 38 months (range 5-70). The overall postoperative leakage rate was 21% (6/29). IntroductionThe idea came for a design where players were stuck a certain section a room as soon as they entered. In a game that's already heavily focused on exploration, this would add a layer of depth. After a week of prototypes, I eventually settled on the idea of having the players enter a "gate" which was located on most hallways. The gate led to a separate room where the player could escape, solve puzzles, and progress to deeper portions of the hallways. In the end, the rooms could be placed just about anywhere in each map, and the design would still work.I've included the original concept sketches above. They are very crude, and not at all what ended up on the finished product. The first design had a large open area with several platforms in the center, as well as a "trap" in the lower right corner of the room. Players would have to travel to the platforms, climb them, and then enter the "trap." The trap would open up to reveal a different room with an exit gate. There would be other rooms throughout where you'd travel to in order to find the exit. The plan for the traps were very simple. Each trap was made up of a pair of platforms connected by a rope.

This has left all eyes and heads at CNN agglut with “how,” not “where.” I understand the argument, but it doesn't seem to me the same as where we're located on this planet.

We are all living on an island, even though it is a tiny speck compared to the universe. We may be at different points on the globe, but the point of this piece is we all occupy the same geographical area.

I know this is one of the things we’re supposed to think about more these days, and it is certainly something to ponder when you’re watching a movie on the big screen or a live sporting event on your TV.

How has this affected the news?

It hasn’t.

And here’s why.

How has this affected the coverage?

It hasn’t.

Let me state for the record that I am in no way saying that the coverage is not any more important, or that the journalists and anchors with great “how” are not doing it more than the anchors with less “how.”
